title: DevOps Engineer (€60k)
description: Some companies use DevOps to ship apps faster. This team is using it to help reshape how future medicines are discovered. We’re working with a fast\-growing deep\-tech company operating at the intersection of AI, computational science, and pharmaceutical innovation. Their platform is tackling one of the biggest challenges in drug development,, using advanced simulation and crystal structure prediction technology to accelerate research that traditionally takes years. Now they’re looking for a DevOps Engineer who loves building systems from the ground up. Not just maintaining infrastructure… but architecting the engine that powers cutting\-edge scientific computing. **This is the kind of role where you’ll:** ⚙️ Build self\-hosted CI/CD systems engineers actually enjoy using 📦 Orchestrate containerised workloads across distributed infrastructure 📊 Design observability stacks that give real\-time visibility into complex systems 🛠 Own infrastructure\-as\-code and automation from day one 🧠 Work directly with highly technical R\&D teams and leadership on scaling architecture 🚀 Help create an environment where experimentation and innovation can move faster, safely They’re not looking for someone who waits for tickets to appear. They want someone curious, hands\-on, and excited by solving hard infrastructure problems in a highly technical environment. **What you’ll likely bring:** • Strong experience in DevOps / Platform Engineering / SRE • Confidence working with Docker, Podman, or container ecosystems • Experience building CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions, GitLab CI/CD, Jenkins, or similar • Hands\-on knowledge of observability and monitoring stacks (Prometheus, Grafana, alerting systems, etc.) • Infrastructure\-as\-Code experience with tools like Ansible • Strong Linux fundamentals and scripting skills • Python proficiency for automation and tooling • A self\-starter mindset with the ability to work closely with technical and non\-technical stakeholders alike **Why this role stands out:** This isn’t another “keep the lights on” DevOps role. You’ll be joining a highly innovative, award\-winning environment where infrastructure directly supports scientific breakthroughs, AI\-driven research, and next\-generation pharmaceutical technology. If you enjoy autonomy, technical depth, and building scalable systems that actually matter, this is worth a conversation.

title: Digital Content Producer
description: **Your role:** We are seeking a creative and dynamic **Digital Content Producer** to develop, create and manage engaging content across our digital platforms. The ideal candidate will be passionate about creating a memorable and exciting brand experience for our audience at all touch points. **Day to day you will:** * Produce engaging online social and digital content, including video, graphics, Reels, TikToks, and Stories, for our social platforms and commercial campaigns. Shoot and edit video for use across multiple platforms. * Manage the station's social media pages, ensuring regular updates throughout the day in line with our social publishing strategy. * Ensure all content meets brand standards and regulations, is relevant, and aligns with the tone and voice of our audiences. * Work with national and local sales teams to generate digital commercial opportunities and deliver great ideas and engaging campaigns. Support cross\-functional teams with their digital content needs. * Manage the publishing and engagement of social media content. * Maintain and update our digital analytics. Use social insights to ensure content is performing effectively and compile regular reports. * Work to ensure a memorable and exciting brand experience at all touch points. **What we are looking for from you:** * Proven experience in creating engaging social media content (video, graphics, etc.). * Proficiency in video shooting and editing. * Strong understanding of social media platforms and trends as well as emerging technologies * Excellent critical thinking skills as well as writing and editing skills. * Experience with social media analytics and reporting. * Ability to work collaboratively with cross\-functional teams. * Strong organisational and time\-management skills. **We are Onic:** Every day our audiences welcome us into their lives and allow us to share their day through. Individually and collectively in every setting and at any time of the day we are their trusted companion. Their trust allows us to connect in a deep and meaningful way across our audio, digital and online platforms. The fusion of the strength of our heritage and our cutting edge innovation enables us to deliver what our communities want, when they want it. We’re about connecting and cultivating communities of loyal listeners, online and on\-air. With a monthly media footprint of over 10 million across on air and digital we have real broadcast coverage across Ireland. A national network that is proudly local and boldly vocal about what makes you – and us – unique. We know when to turn up the volume and when to dial it back. We’re all about celebrating local culture while staying tuned in to global trends. Our fresh, inclusive outlook means we can meet the needs of our local yet diverse communities while still connecting with a savvy, worldly audience. For more check out Onic.ie **Life at News** Driven by passion, guided by principles and acting with purpose. We represent, reflect and reach the nation, telling the stories that matter. We inform our audiences so that they can make decisions based on trusted information. We represent, reflect and reach the nation, telling the stories that matter most. To ensure these stories resonate with as broad an audience as possible, it is essential that our organisation reflects the diversity of the people we reach. Whether through age, gender, ethnicity, disability, social class or sexuality, we are committed to representing the rich variety of voices that make up our society. And we champion a culture where everyone has the opportunity to contribute and thrive as we continue to innovate and drive long term sustainable growth. This is done through our Diversity Strategy. At News UK, we take pride in our exceptional employee\-led networks that bring together individuals with shared interests and create a vibrant sense of community. We believe our networks play a vital role in fostering a collaborative and supportive work culture at News UK. Groups that we support include the African \& Caribbean Network, Parents and Carers, and LGBTQ\+ group News Is Out, and the Apprentice Society. **Benefits** Some of our benefits include: * ‘Bikes for Work’ ’ scheme * Up to 60% discount on Harper Collins books * Maternity leave up to 18 weeks full basic salary \& paternity leave up to 2 weeks * Access to exclusive events and competitions with exciting brands such as talkSPORT, Virgin Radio UK \& The Times. Weekly virtual panel chats with top journalists and celebrities * Access to wellbeing benefits such as EAP, physio/massage and counselling * A generous pension scheme with employer contributions of up to 5% * Wide range of training available, plus full LinkedIn Learning access * 25 days holiday, plus bank holidays and up to 4 volunteering days per year We are committed to providing an inclusive recruitment process. If you require reasonable adjustments due to a disability or health condition at any stage of your application or interview, please let us know. You can contact us at careers@news.co.uk to discuss how we can support you to perform at your best.

title: Electrical Apprentice - Field Service Engineer
description: At ABB, we help industries outrun \- leaner and cleaner. Here, progress is an expectation \- for you, your team, and the world. As a global market leader, we’ll give you what you need to make it happen. It won’t always be easy, growing takes grit. But at ABB, you’ll never run alone. Run what runs the world. This Position reports to: Service Team Leader In the Electrical Apprentice Field Service Engineer role, you will have the opportunity to support business, collaborate with different teams, and contribute to the company's strategic objectives. We are looking for an energetic and enthusiastic person to work with our field service team within the Electrification Services Division. We have a highly qualified and experienced team of Field Service Engineers and are now looking to train the next generation of Field Service Engineers. Training will be provided locally in Ireland. The work model for the role is: \#Onsite from our Dublin location This role is contributing to the Electrification Services Division. You will be highly trained to work in the fast\-paced Electrification market supporting ABB equipment installed base. The initial training will be broad\-based and give a solid foundation in electrical engineering and understanding of the ABB business and approach to the market. Upon completion of the apprenticeship, you will be proficient in the following areas: * Commissioning and start\-up, Maintenance activities, Diagnostics \& repair, Understanding 3 phase and 1 phase electrical systems. * Participating in formal training to develop personal and leadership skills in preparation for future professional roles. * Contributing to diverse social events and team activities and using the chance to establish a broad network within ABB **Qualifications For The Role** * Hold a genuine interest in electrical engineering and a career in the engineering sector. * Leaving Certificate in 6 subjects, preferably minimum pass in ordinary Math’s. Strong written and verbal communication skills. Proficiency in Microsoft Office. * Be able to work independently and as part of a team with a logical and problem\-solving mindset. Full Clean Driving License as travel to both customers and ABB sites will be required as part of the role. More about us As part of our dedication to our employees, ABB Ireland offers an attractive array of benefits which include: * Salary * Paid overtime * Annual leave entitlement * On the job Training In an ever\-changing world, industry is facing fluctuating energy and operating costs and an insecure energy supply while demand for electrification continues to increase. At the same time, companies are under pressure to cut emissions and evolve their business models as their electrical assets get older and available investment is shrinking. ABB Electrification Service partners with utility, industrial and commercial customers across all industries to manage their energy and electrical infrastructure as strategic operational assets in a safe, smart, and sustainable way. We value people from diverse backgrounds. Could this be your story? Apply today or visit www.abb.com to read more about us and learn about the impact of our solutions across the globe. \#MyABBStory We value people from different backgrounds. Could this be your story? Apply today or visit www.abb.com to read more about us and learn about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

title: Electrical Day Technician
description: **Background:** Veolia is Ireland’s leading environmental services company. We provide a comprehensive range of energy, waste and water solutions and are dedicated to carbon reduction, protecting the environment and building the circular economy. We offer all employees a benefits package as standard which includes full VHI cover, pension and education assistance. When you see the world as we do, you see the chance to help the world take better care of its resources, and help it become a better place for everyone. It's why we're looking for someone who's just as committed as we are, to push for genuine change and bring our ambition of Ecological Transformation to life. We know that everyone here at Veolia can help us work alongside our communities, look after the environment, and contribute to our inclusive culture. **Electrical Day Technician** **Contract Duration/Type:** **Permanent** **Location: Clondalki, Dublin** **Hours:** **Monday\-Thursday 07:30 to 16:00 Friday 07:30 to 15:30** **Overview of the role:** The Lead Technicians will train on all Processes and techniques across Predictive Maintenance, CMMS (Pemac), Reliability techniques,HV and LV distribution, Turbines, Boilers Compressors, Diesel Generators Pumps, The working hours are Monday\-Thursday 07:30 to 16:00 Friday 07:30 to 15:30 The day lead technician may be required from time to time to back fill on shift, and may also be required to support the site in emergency situations outside of normal working hours. **Duties of the Role include:** * Coordinate the day to day work with shift technicians on\-site particularly morning shift changeover * Be the point of contact for service providers * Trouble\-shooting and high\-level investigation of utility plant equipment faults and failures – root cause analysis * Compliance/quality engineering – recording and investigation of deviations on utility plant and equipment * Carry out planned preventive, predictive and corrective maintenance as required * Ensure PMs are completed on time * Good knowledge and understanding of CMMS systems * Develop PMs and Job Plans as well as SOPs for plant equipment. * Liaising with site planner re stores to identify spare part requirements; * Review maintenance programme periodically to ensure optimal uptime levels are achieved * Support and comply with all Safety procedures * Experience with: HV and LV distribution. Electrical generation turbines, diesel generators, boilers and Fuel Oil storage and distribution systems. Industrial gas installations * Be able to take a leadership role in decision\-making as regards to plant operation and equipment suitability for operation; * Have sufficient knowledge of the plant to allow decision making in terms of taking plant off line, communicating with relevant people on site and be capable of identifying the action required to get plant back on line or introduce back\-up and standby systems; * Understand the actions to be taken in the event of a serious service outage; * Provide leadership and mentoring to the team in developing their capability to resolve complex plant deviations and failures * Provide leadership and mentoring to team members in developing their capability to resolve complex plant deviations and failures * Support the Site engineer \& HV Engineer in carrying out Root Cause Analysis on plant failures to determine the sequence of events, the underlying root cause and assist the in developing proposals to remedy the fault; * Provide support to capital improvement projects on utilities systems, and be involved in the commissioning and hand over from projects to operations. * Assist the Site Engineer \& HV Engineer in planning and executing complex utility service isolations for maintenance, projects and client works; * Identify, develop and, if approved, implement Continuous Improvement measures to improve the efficiency of plant and working practices; * Seek to continuously improve knowledge and understanding of complex mechanical systems; **Required skills for this role:** * Analytical mindset * Experience with six sigma/lean preferred * Previous experience required, craft qualified electrician * Past working experience ideally gained in energy conversion and distribution **Please note:** If you are an EU/EEA national, you will be asked to show proof of right to work in Ireland. If you are a non\-EU/EEA national , you will require current and valid permission to work and reside in the Republic of Ireland. Information on legislation and guides to the procedures in relation to obtaining greencard permits ,work permits, spousal/dependant permits is available on the Department of Jobs, Enterprise and Innovation website.

title: Electrical Engineer
description: Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions. We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Scope of the role:** The Electrical Engineer will be a key member of the Project Team. You will have notable cross\-discipline design and technical experience in addition to the ability to communicate and engage with stakeholders, at all levels as required. This will include managing design packages from initial review, through to assisting with estimation, procurement, planning and handover for execution on site. With the ability to support the end\-to\-end project deliverables at all stages, the Electrical Engineer will be integral to the delivery of high valued projects for major data centre clients. You will be highly motivated to succeed in a dynamic, high\-pressure, and fast evolving environment. A strong interest and curiosity in all aspects of the construction industry is expected. The role is responsible for both technical engineering knowledge as well as assistance in management of project team deliverables. A key part of the role is the need to prioritise, co\-ordinate and to successfully deliver compliant building facilities. In addition to working as part of a high\-performance team, the Electrical Engineer will hold responsibility for effectively supporting the Engineering Manager, as well as Engineers within Central Engineering, and Electrical Managers and Engineers on site, to ensure that relationships are maintained, expectations are met, and Mercury Engineering’s interests are upheld. **Key Responsibilities of the Role:** **Team Management:** * Collaborate with engineers, and construction managers to develop comprehensive project plans and ensure delivery on schedule. * Collaborate with any other consultants, subcontractors, supervisors, planners, estimators, quantity surveyors and the general workforce involved in the project. * Liaise with clients and other 3rd parties as required, such as insurance companies, vendors, etc. * Develop comprehensive scope\-of\-works (SoW), task information deliverables lists (TIDP), and responsibility matrices (R\&R/ RACI) for Electrical disciplines. * Raise any design\-related risks to planning, commercial and management, and establish a course to minimise their impact. * Maintain accurate records of key design activities (e.g. changes, issues) as they arise and escalate to management as appropriate. * Assist the Central Engineering and site teams with proactive contributions to construction and team matters. * Attend design related meetings and provide continual input for optimisation. * Collaborate on new design issues/ changes/ research within the team. Assist with new tenders and PCSA (pre\-construction services agreements) deliverables within the Business Unit. * **Project Management \& Planning:** * Oversee the construction process to ensure adherence to design specifications and schedules. * Assist in developing design delivery schedules along with the client, project manager, design team and consultants. * Assist with managing the deliverables and approvals processes of design packages to ensure full approval for procurement. * Check design programmes on projects are aligned to consultant resource and construction/ procurement requirements. * Conduct site visits and report progress and quality of work. Correctly follow information control and flow standards e.g. for RFIs, use of ACC, etc. * **Technical Engineering:** * Ensure Electrical technical compliance on all projects. * Ensure knowledge of client and country\-specific design standards. * Assess and mitigate potential risks and issues as they’re identified in design, or that could have potential impact on construction activities. * Review design proposals by others for suitability and constructability. * Offer proposals for added value (VE) and off\-site manufacturing (OSM). * Ensure material choices are aligned to project requirements, budget, and QEHS standards. * Prepare comprehensive handover packs to the site team for any packages under your responsibility. * Coordinate and assist with coordination of Electrical disciplines and interfaces with CSA and Mechanical. * Check plans, drawings, and quantities for accuracy of calculations. * Review, monitor, and interpret the contract design documents supplied by the client or architect. **Sustainability:** * Integrate sustainable practices into design and construction processes. * Evaluate the environmental impact of projects and propose options for improvements. **Documentation:** * Prepare detailed reports, and documentation for project approval and permits. * Maintain accurate records of project progress and changes. * Manage key registers throughout the project design period: + Procurement schedule. + TIDP/ MIDP. + RFI log. + Deviation tracker. + Lessons Learnt log. + Issues, interface \& scope gap trackers. + Design change tracker. + Value Engineering Register OSM Register + **Other:** * Provide technical guidance and support to construction teams. * Review and submit technical submittals. * Assist with recruitment activities for the Central Engineering team as deemed suitable. * Undertake other engineering tasks or duties as required by the Engineering Manager and/ or Company. * The Company reserves the right to vary the scope and nature of the Employee’s normal duties to consider changes in the Company’s work patterns and practices and the requirements of its business. **Essential Criteria for the Role:** * Minimum 5 years post graduate experience in the Industrial, Pharmaceutical, Life Sciences or Data Centre sectors. * Demonstration of design, technical and construction knowledge is essential for this role. * Experience in leading meetings and coordinating the other team members. * Strong working knowledge of design and analysis software such as BIM, Revit. * Clean driving licence essential ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer*****.**

title: Embedded Consultant - GSOC Operator - Junior Analysts
description: Control Risks has been providing outsourced embedded consulting management for over 40 years, with our support allowing clients to operate successfully, utilising a wide range of expertise. Our embedded consultant service program allows our consultants to be integrated into the client's organisation and provide our services from individual client sites. We are a preferred partner to our clients because of our ethical practices, security intelligence, practical know\-how and management support provided throughout our diverse services lines available throughout our global network of offices. With our global presence and extensive experience, Control Risks maintains an ever\-growing database of multi\-national risk management professionals to match skills and knowledge to meet our clients ever increasing needs. We are experiencing demanding growth across our Embedded Consultant Services (ECS) program and are seeking to build our existing database of embedded consultants across EMEA. We are seeking consultants for a number of roles in our clients GSOC in Dublin, Ireland. The GSOC is a part of an integrated global structure with standardized processes and systems. **Requirements** We are seeking experienced professionals to support our client's Global Security Operations Centres (GSOC). The GSOC plays a critical role in protecting people, operations, and assets globally through real\-time monitoring, analysis, and response. We are actively hiring for the following positions: **GSOC Operator (Junior Analyst) \- (24/7 shifts)** Responsibilities: * Monitor, assess, and report on global threats and regional risks. * Conduct open\-source and proprietary intelligence analysis. * Perform system audits and assist in response planning. * Support enterprise\-level data analysis and security assessments. Requirements: * Experience in risk analysis, intelligence, or security monitoring * Skilled in OSINT tools, databases, and incident management platforms * Clear, concise report writing and stakeholder communication. Requirements: * 1\+ year GSOC supervisory experience * Strong leadership, communication, and decision\-making skills * Technical knowledge of alarm/CCTV, risk monitoring, and OSINT * Ability to remain calm and direct teams during high\-pressure events. Apply Now and be part of a growing GSOC team that safeguards people and operations worldwide. Submit your resume and specify in which role(s) you are interested. **Benefits** Control Risks offers a competitive package commensurate with the role and region.

title: Employee Benefits Consultant
description: **Description** --------------- This is an exciting opportunity for an experienced professional to design, implement, and manage health, risk and wellbeing programmes, delivering best\-in\-class advice, strengthening insurer relationships, and ensuring full compliance with Central Bank of Ireland regulatory requirements. As a Senior Consultant – Health, Risk \& Wellbeing, you will play a key leadership role within the Irish Health \& Benefits practice, providing high\-quality consulting, broking, and advisory services to a diverse portfolio of mid\-market clients, driving impactful outcomes and client success. **The Role** Client Consulting \& Advisory * Act as lead consultant on a portfolio of Irish health, risk and wellbeing clients * Provide strategic advice on group risk (Life, SPDIS, GIP, Critical Illness), group health insurance and ancillary benefits * Design and review employee benefit strategies aligned to client objectives and workforce needs * Analyse claims experience, premium trends and management information to support recommendations * Prepare and deliver high‑quality client reports, renewal reviews and executive summaries * Deliver clear, practical recommendations supported by data‑driven insights Renewals, Underwriting \& Insurer Engagement * Project manage end‑to‑end renewal processes, ensuring accuracy, timeliness and strong governance * Oversee underwriting, claims and data submissions to insurers * Lead renewal meetings, tender processes and insurer negotiations on behalf of clients * Challenge insurer terms and pricing to achieve optimal outcomes for clients * Build and maintain strong insurer relationships to secure competitive and flexible terms * Ensure scheme administration related to renewals, claims and underwriting is kept up to date Client Relationship Management * Develop and maintain trusted senior relationships with key client stakeholders * Act as primary escalation point for complex client queries and issues * Ensure delivery of a consistently high standard of client service * Retain and grow existing client relationships through proactive engagement Growth, Collaboration \& Leadership * Identify opportunities to extend services across existing client portfolios * Support new business pitches, tenders and prospect meetings * Work collaboratively with colleagues in Retirement, Reward and wider advisory teams * Mentor junior consultants and support the development of team capability * Contribute to process improvement and best‑practice initiatives across the practice Compliance \& Professional Standards * Ensure all advice and documentation complies with Central Bank of Ireland regulations and the Consumer Protection Code * Adhere to GDPR and data protection requirements at all times * Maintain accurate records and activity updates on internal CRM systems * Complete all required CPD and maintain professional accreditation * Uphold internal quality and excellence standards **Qualifications** ------------------ **What you’ll bring** Experience \& Qualifications: * Qualified Financial Adviser (QFA) and/or APA (mandatory) * Proven experience in a client‑facing health, risk or wellbeing consulting role within the Irish market * Strong technical knowledge of group risk and/or group health insurance products * Experience managing complex corporate client relationships and renewals * Strong understanding of Irish regulatory and compliance obligations * Experience advising large or multinational clients * Commercial awareness with a track record of supporting client retention and growth * Experience mentoring or supporting the development of junior colleagues Skills \& Competencies * Strong consultative, analytical and problem‑solving skills * Excellent written and verbal communication and presentation capability * Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced environment * Confident, professional and collaborative working style **What we offer** At WTW Ireland, your wellbeing comes first. Depending on your role, you'll receive 25 days of annual leave with the option to add 5 extra days—giving you plenty of time to rest and recharge. You'll also benefit from a €1,500 health insurance allowance, life assurance, future\-focused pension contributions, hybrid working options, educational support and a dedicated volunteer day. Customise additional flexible benefits with extras like our share scheme, cycle\-to\-work programme, and enhanced optical cover. Start making the most of your career and wellbeing with a range of benefits tailored for you. **Equal Opportunity Employer** We’re committed to equal employment opportunity and provide application, interview and workplace adjustments and accommodations to all applicants. If you foresee any barriers, from the application process through to joining WTW, please email candidatehelpdesk@wtwco.com (H\&B1\)

title: Employee Relations Associate
description: **The Rehab Group** We are an independent voluntary organisation providing services and support for people with disabilities and their families for more than 70 years. Our work positively impacts the lives of over 12,000 people with disabilities, equipping them with the confidence, skills, and self\-belief to achieve their expressed goals **We Welcome All Backgrounds and Abilities!** We are an inclusive employer and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds, including those with different abilities. We are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible work environment for all individuals. If you require any reasonable accommodations during the application process or once employed, please let us know. Examples of accommodations we can provide include modified workstations, flexible work hours, assistive technologies, or adjustments to communication methods during interviews or in the workplace. Our goal is to ensure that all candidates and employees have the support they need to succeed in their roles. **Employee Relations Associate** **Park West Business Park,** **Park West, Dublin 12 (Hyrbrid Model: 2 days office\-based, 3 days from home)** *Permanent Contract, 39 hours* **Job Purpose** Employee Relations Associate is responsible for ensuring that the employee relations agenda is met and delivered upon within the Rehab Group. **Minimum Education \& Skills required** HR / Business third level qualification ( **NFQ8** ) and CIPD (Member of the Chartered Institute of Personnel Development) qualified (or equivalent). **Job Duties \& Responsibilities** Minimum 2 years Employee Relations experience gained within a multisite and fast paced environment and accustomed to high volumes of work. A strong foundation in employment law and industrial relations experience. Relevant and up to date understanding of Employment Legislation in Ireland and Europe. Strong negotiation, conflict resolution and mediation skills are essential. Excellent knowledge of Human Resources best practices including Performance Management, Employee Relations / Engagement. **Additional requirements** Excellent knowledge of Human Resources best practices including Performance Management, Employee Relations / Engagement. Problem Solving and Decision Making skills. Ability to work to tight deadlines and handle multiple priorities simultaneously. High attention to detail. **Staff Benefits** \* Company Pension Scheme \* Paid maternity/paternity/adoption leave \* Educational leave \* 27 days annual leave \* Bike to work scheme \* Tax saver travel scheme \* Income protection \* Unpaid leave \* Health and Wellbeing programme \* Company credit union scheme \* Employee discount scheme \* Digital Doctor \* Financial support for studying and study leave \* Long Service Reward Scheme \* sick leave \* Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) **\*Rewarding Career \*Career with meaning \*attractive benefits \*work life balance\*** **To view full extensive job description please, click apply to visit our company site and download attachment at the bottom of the page.** ***These duties and responsibilities are a reflection of the present service requirements and may be subject to review and amendment to meet the changing needs of the services*** \*.\* **Closing Date: Wednesday, 21st April 2026\.** *The Rehab Group is an equal opportunity employer*

title: Engineering Graduate 2026
description: **Discover your exciting role** We are seeking highly motivated **Engineering Graduates** to join our talented team at Exyte. As an Engineering Graduate, you will have the opportunity to work alongside experienced engineers and gain hands\-on experience in delivering cutting\-edge projects. You will play an integral role in various stages of project design, development, and implementation, while contributing to the creation of state\-of\-the\-art facilities. For our 2026 Graduate Programme, we are recruiting for the following **engineering** disciplines: * Mechanical * Electrical * BIM/Design * Civil/Structural * Architectural * Process * Quality * Commissioning **Explore Your Tasks And Responsibilities** * Assist in the design, planning, and execution of engineering projects within our sectors of expertise. * Support senior engineers in preparing technical documentation, drawings, and specifications. * Participate in site visits and contribute to project assessments and feasibility studies. * Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to ensure seamless integration of engineering systems. * Develop and maintain project schedules, budgets, and risk management plans. * Learn and apply industry standards, safety regulations, and best practices to all tasks. * Continuously expand your knowledge of engineering processes and systems, with mentorship and guidance from experienced engineers. **Show Your Expertise** **Who You Are:** * A final year student or recent graduate with a relevant engineering degree. * Passionate about engineering and technology with a strong desire to build a successful career in the industry. * Excellent problem\-solving skills, with a keen eye for detail and the ability to think critically. * Strong communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to collaborate effectively in a team environment. * Able to prioritize tasks, work independently, and meet deadlines. * Enthusiastic, adaptable, and eager to learn in a fast\-paced, innovative environment. **What We Offer** * Competitive salary and benefits package, including healthcare \& pension. * Travel \& relocation opportunities across mainland Europe. * A dynamic and supportive work environment with opportunities for professional growth. * Exposure to innovative projects and advanced engineering technologies. * Mentorship and training programs designed to help you develop your technical and leadership skills. * A chance to work with some of the brightest minds in the industry.

title: Enterprise Business Development Representative
description: Fin is the AI Customer Agent company on a mission to help businesses provide perfect customer experiences. Our AI Agent Fin is the highest\-performing AI Customer Agent on the market today, enabling businesses to deliver impeccable, always\-on customer support across the customer journey – from service, to sales, to ecommerce. Powered by our own AI models, Fin resolves complex customer issues end\-to\-end across every channel, with minimal set\-up and integration. Fin can also be combined with our natively integrated Intercom help desk for one single system that is designed to meet the needs of modern day support teams. Founded in 2011, Fin became one of the fastest growing companies and remains one of the largest private software companies in the world with nearly 30,000 global businesses using our products to transform their customer support. Driven by our core values, we push boundaries, build with speed and intensity, and relentlessly deliver incredible value to our customers. **What's the opportunity?** --------------------------- As an Enterprise Business Development Representative, you will play a critical role in driving revenue growth for the Fin Sales organization through strategic pipeline generation within our enterprise segment. Based in our Dublin HQ, you will be the tip of the spear for Fin's expansion across EMEA, engaging with senior stakeholders at large, complex organizations and helping them see how Fin can transform their customer experience. We invest in our BDRs. We strongly believe in giving our team the opportunities to grow \& develop at Fin, to push themselves outside of their comfort zones to achieve goals they never thought possible. In joining the Business Development team at Fin, you join a community that believes in development and promotion from within. We're looking for people who are excited to work in a fast\-moving company, make an impact, and rapidly develop their career in enterprise sales. As a BDR team, we empower one another to achieve our goals, exceed expectations and continue to deliver real business impact. title: Environmental Compliance Specialist | West Dublin | Onsite
description: **ENVIRONMENTAL COMPLIANCE SPECIALIST \| WEST DUBLIN \| ONSITE** We have partnered with Recycling \& Waste Management Company of the Year, dedicated to innovative recycling solutions and sustainable practices. Based in Dublin 12, they provide commercial waste and other related services to businesses in Ireland. We are looking to recruit an Environmental Compliance Specialist for an immediate start. The role represents an exciting opportunity for an ambitious individual to develop and expand their range of business skills in a rapidly expanding business with over 130 employees. You will be part of the management team in an interesting and diverse role helping to drive improvements across all departments in a very progressive group. **Role \& Responsibilities:** * Ensure continued compliance with all relevant waste, transport licences and permits * Ensure continued compliance with all relevant health \& safety legislation * Conduct on\-site sampling, monitoring and reporting in accordance with licence conditions * Keep up to date with all new and upcoming relevant legislation * Maintain accurate records and prepare reports, letters, and memorandums with respect to compliance matters including monthly and annual returns to EPA, TFS, CSO, County Councils, REPAK * Management of the 3 ISO certifications, Environment, Health \& Safety and Quality driving continual improvement of each. * Continuous training of employees in the procedures and systems in conjunction with HR and other department managers. * Liaise with members of the public, professional bodies, customers and suppliers on compliance \& legal related issues and training of customers in good waste management practices * Conducting site audits and taking responsibility to ensure all issues are addressed and closed out appropriately with the relevant managers. * Create and manage customer waste reports including system checking of the accuracy of the weighing information and working with other departments to continually improve the systems and reporting functionality * Assist business development with proposals \& tenders as required. **Skills \& Qualifications:** * Qualified environmental or health \& safety specialist, waste industry experience an advantage but not essential * Relevant third level qualification * Strong system skills including Excel and other business applications * Ability to work to deadlines and prioritisation of key tasks * Ability to work across business units and report to owner managers. * Good communicator, comfortable liaising with and training employees and customers For further information please contact Orla Brennan 086 1720090 / orla.brennan@prismexecutive.ie

title: Executive Assistant - fixed term contract
description: **Line of Service** Internal Firm Services **Industry/Sector** Not Applicable **Specialism** IFS \- Internal Firm Services \- Other **Management Level** Associate **Job Description \& Summary** PwC Ireland\- Bring Your Talent, Grow Your Skills, Unlock Your Potential PwC offers an exciting opportunity to be part of a fast\-growing team of Executive Assistants (EAs) within the Core Administration Hub. Supported in part by the Business Administration Hubs, the EA will provide strategic and professional senior administrative support to designated Firm Partner(s). PwC’s Business Enablement Services (BES) Line of Service combines our support teams from across the firm who play pivotal roles in enabling our business delivery. Within BES, the Business Administration (BA) function delivers innovative workplace and business\-focused solutions across the firm through our collaborative team of experts. Our team ensures the effective operation and support of client service teams is in safe hands. We are passionate about customer service and deliver a wide range of services, from core administrative tasks to value\-add work for our stakeholders. This is a fixed term contract. BA is composed of four Hubs – Core Administration, Commercial Operations, Events and Travel, and Documentation. **This is a fantastic opportunity for the right individual to;** * Be an integral part of the Business Administration team providing dedicated support to Partners * Proactively develop their personal and technical skills to enhance their career * Expand their networks across PwC * Significantly broaden their understanding of the firm To be successful you will have the ability to liaise, influence and challenge effectively to build strong networks and relationships at all levels, both internally and externally. You will report into a Team Leader and will have a Career Coach to support your career aspirations and personal development. You will drive your own self development through gaining continuous feedback, evidencing an active commitment to upskilling in the role and taking ownership to plan and attend regular Career Coach Catch Ups. The role requires a high\-level of self\-management and personal responsibility coupled with the ability to prioritise, organise and manage tasks and workload. You will have a customer service mindset, good technical skills and the ability to keep up to date with current and emerging technology. **Purpose\-led work you’ll be part of** As an Associate, you’ll work as part of a team of problem solvers, helping to solve complex business issues from strategy to execution. Responsibilities include but are not limited to: **Responsible for:** * Management of an executive\-level BA service for Partners * Ownership of all enquiries and problems, resolving or escalating issues as appropriate. * Work with your stakeholders to ensure compliance with the Firm’s policies and procedures and meet the Firm deadlines. * Draft complex and sensitive communications on behalf of partners. * Proactively coordinate meeting logistics and anticipate conflicts. * Attend meetings as appropriate, providing detailed and actionable minutes. * Manage follow\-up on key action items and ensure accountability. * Project coordination \- attend project meetings (as requested) to understand requirements, delivery timescales, supporting actions and regular interaction with the project team. * Manage partner inboxes, prioritising critical communications. Handle sensitive documents with a high level of confidentiality and integrity. * Build and maintain strong client relationships, addressing issues proactively. * Liaise with the BA Hubs and Regional Offices as necessary. * Delegate transactional work where appropriate to effectively manage workloads. * Ability to draft stakeholder emails and engage with stakeholders to make decisions. * Support stakeholders and others in upskilling of Firmwide systems. **Additional responsibilities where required:** * Informal coaching junior team members * Involvement in team activities that contribute to personal \& Firm development * Other ad hoc responsibilities as required **Essential skills \& experience** * Secondary level education and a relevant Post second level qualification. * A minimum of 4 years of proven experience in a similar role, providing comprehensive administrative support to senior executives or management teams. * Proficiency in Microsoft 365 applications (Word, Outlook, PowerPoint, Excel). * Demonstrated strong project management and coordination skills, with the ability to effectively prioritise and manage multiple stakeholders' conflicting priorities. * Experience in handling sensitive information. * Ability to thrive in a fast\-paced, dynamic environment, demonstrating resilience and adaptability. * Demonstrated champion of Evolved PwC Professional behaviours. **Preferred Experience** * Excellent organisational skills and self\-motivation, demonstrating a commitment to continuous self\-development. * Strong influencing and communication skills, both verbal and written. * Ability to develop internal and external networks to support business objectives. * Flexibility and agility to adapt to changes and contribute effectively to system improvements or firm initiatives. * Role model for inclusivity, with an understanding of cultural differences and a commitment to diversity. **Unlock your potential with PwC Ireland** We believe that challenges are better solved together! We’re inspiring and empowering our people to change the world. Powered by the latest technology, you’ll be a part of amazing teams encouraged to collaborate and innovate in a way that creates powerful solutions and makes a positive impact. This purpose\-led work, and our continuous development and encouragement, will help unlock your potential and take your career to the next level. **Enjoy PwC’s perks** We reward your impact, and support your wellbeing, through a competitive compensation package, inclusive employee benefits and flexibility programs that will help you thrive in work and life. Learn more about us at Life@PwC. (https://www.pwc.ie/careers\-ie/life\-at\-pwc.html). **Being appreciated for being you** Our most valuable asset is our people and we grow stronger as we learn from one another. We are an equal opportunity employer and we value diversity. title: Fabricator
description: Cental is a global supplier of fully designed, engineered, built and integrated modular and skid\-mounted solutions for the Data Centre, Utilities, Life Sciences, and Oil \& Gas markets. From our headquarters in Carlow, Ireland, we deliver comprehensive solutions for clients worldwide. Our people are the foundation of our success. With three manufacturing facilities and a talented team of engineers, designers, project managers, and tradespeople, our team manages every detail, ensuring seamless delivery from initial design to final installation. We value teamwork, respect, and continuous improvement, creating a collaborative, innovative environment where everyone can reach their potential. We also believe in celebrating achievements and recognising effort. From monthly quality and safety initiatives for breakfast treats and local tool vouchers, to company breakfasts, staff BBQs, and occasional outings, we bring people together to enjoy achievements and strengthen our team spirit. Growth and learning are central to Cental. Whether you’re joining as an apprentice, graduate, or experienced professional, we provide training and development opportunities to help you build a rewarding, long\-term career. If you’re passionate about excellence, innovation, and teamwork, join us and be part of a company that’s engineering solutions and building futures. Cental is seeking a Fabricator who will be responsible for cutting, shaping, assembling, and welding metal components to produce high‑quality steel structures, frames, and modular components in line with engineering drawings, quality standards, and safety requirements. The role supports high‑volume manufacturing in a fast‑paced environment, ensuring accuracy, efficiency, and consistent product quality. **Key Responsibilities** * Interpret and work from technical drawings, blueprints, and fabrication plans. * Fabricate steel components using cutting, grinding, drilling, forming, and welding processes. * Assemble structural components ensuring accurate dimensions and tolerances. * Operate fabrication equipment such as: + MIG/Stick welding machines + Plasma cutters + Saws (band saw, chop saw) + Press brakes and rollers + Grinders and power tools * Conduct weld preparation, finishing, and quality checks. * Ensure welds meet internal quality standards and any relevant codes. * Inspect finished products for defects or deviations. * Complete rework where required. * Ensure compliance with quality procedures, ISO standards, and project specifications. * Adhere strictly to company safety policies, including PPE requirements. * Work collaboratively with engineering, production, and assembly teams. **Skills \& Experience** * Proven experience as a Fabricator in a manufacturing or engineering environment. * Strong ability to read and interpret fabrication and engineering drawings. * Skilled in MIG welding. * Competent in measuring, marking out, and working to tight tolerances. * Experience using fabrication workshop tools and machinery. * Strong attention to detail and commitment to quality. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

title: Facilities Specialist - Tech Environment

It's a great fit for someone who's into technology and the gaming industry, enjoys solving problems, and likes being hands\-on in a fast\-paced setting. What you'll be doing \-Act as a go\-to person for workplace issues, making sure requests are handled quickly and efficiently \-Keep the office in great shape, from general upkeep to making sure systems and equipment are working properly \-Help with office moves, desk setups, and keeping floor plans and records up to date \-Keep an eye on building systems (like HVAC and BMS), troubleshoot issues, and escalate when needed \-Work closely with building management and technical teams to keep everything running smoothly \-Use internal systems to track requests, assets, and general facilities activity \-Manage the helpdesk, making sure all tickets are logged, followed up, and closed on time \-Support health \& safety standards and make sure processes are being followed \-Assist with admin tasks like raising POs and processing invoices \-Coordinate contractors and vendors, including scheduling, access, and overseeing work when required \-Keep track of equipment, assets, and warranties \-Work with different teams to solve problems and keep things moving \-Look for ways to improve how things are done, especially using tech and systems \-Help manage day\-to\-day office needs like supplies and on\-site services \-Get involved in projects and any other tasks that come up What we're looking for \-Some experience in Facilities, Workplace, or Operations \-A genuine interest in technology and modern workplaces \-An interest in or passion for the gaming industry is a big plus \-Familiarity with facilities or helpdesk systems (Maximo, Concept, or similar) is helpful \-Basic understanding of building systems (HVAC, BMS, etc.) is an advantage \-Someone who's practical, proactive, and enjoys solving problems \-Strong communication skills and a customer\-first mindset \-Ability to juggle multiple tasks and stay organised in a busy environment \-Comfortable using spreadsheets and working with data \-Awareness of Health \& Safety practices is a plus \-A team player who's flexible and ready to adapt as things change Adecco Ireland is acting as an Employment Agency in relation to this vacancy.

title: Finance Business Partner
description: Job Purpose: **About RWS** RWS is a global AI solutions company empowering the world’s most trusted enterprise AI. Our proprietary Cultural Intelligence Layer, powered by 250,000 data specialists, cultural and language experts and deep domain professionals, backed by 45\+ patents, makes enterprise AI culturally fluent, contextually accurate and secure, ensuring every interaction reflects a brand’s tone, context and customer values. Through our Generate, Transform and Protect segments, we deliver intelligent content, enterprise knowledge, large\-scale localization and IP protection for global growth. Trusted by 80\+ of the world’s top 100 brands, RWS provides the confidence, governance and expertise organizations need to deploy AI safely, responsibly and at scale. Headquartered in the UK, RWS is listed on AIM (RWS.L). More information: rws.com. **About Content Tech** Content Tech is a division within RWS that develops and supports a portfolio of content management, structured authoring, and digital experience products. Our portfolio includes Tridion Docs, Tridion Sites, Tridion One, FontoSDK, Contenta, TimeArc, and LWB360, serving enterprise clients across regulated and complex content environments worldwide. The Finance team within Content Tech provides commercial, strategic, and operational finance support to the business, working closely with Group Finance, the Shared Service Centre, and divisional stakeholders to deliver accurate, insightful reporting and maintain a robust control environment. Job Overview: **Job Purpose** This role is the **Finance Business Partner** for Content Tech, responsible for owning the end\-to\-end budget and forecasting cycle, delivering insightful management reporting, and acting as a trusted finance partner to divisional leadership. Working alongside the Senior Finance Business Partner, this position drives financial performance through rigorous analysis, variance investigation, and forward\-looking financial planning across the CT product portfolio. The role also ensures the accuracy of transactional postings, including revenue recognition, and works in partnership with the Shared Service Centre and Group Controllership to improve processes and strengthen controls. You will contribute to strategic projects such as M\&A activity and three\-year planning, making this a role that combines hands\-on financial management with commercial and strategic exposure. **Key Responsibilities** **Budget and Forecasting** * Own the end\-to\-end budget and forecast process for Content Tech, partnering with product and commercial stakeholders to build credible, well\-evidenced financial plans * Prepare and maintain rolling forecasts, ensuring assumptions are clearly documented and regularly challenged against actuals * Coordinate divisional submissions into the Group planning cycle, including the annual budget, quarterly reforecasts, and three\-year plan inputs * Build and maintain financial models across the CT product portfolio to support scenario analysis, investment cases, and resource allocation decisions * Track forecast accuracy over time and implement improvements to the forecasting methodology where gaps are identified **Reporting, Analysis, and Variance Investigation** * Prepare monthly and quarterly management reporting packs for divisional leadership, with clear commentary on performance against forecast * Identify, investigate, and explain variances across revenue, cost, and margin lines, distinguishing between timing, mix, and underlying performance drivers * Develop and maintain KPI dashboards that give leadership a real\-time view of commercial and financial performance * Support the preparation of Board and ELT materials, ensuring figures are accurate, internally consistent, and clearly presented **Transactional Accuracy and Revenue Recognition** * Review transactional data posted by the SSC and Commercial Analyst, including revenue recognition entries, to ensure accuracy and completeness * Oversee the correct application of revenue recognition policies across licence types (perpetual, term, SaaS) in line with IFRS 15 and Group policy * Liaise with Financial Controllers and the SSC on any queries arising during close, ensuring the division meets its close timetable obligations * Be the first point of contact for audit queries relating to the Content Tech division, coordinating responses and gathering supporting evidence as required **Automation and Process Improvement** * Identify and drive opportunities to automate repetitive financial processes, reducing manual effort and error rates * Partner with the SSC, Group Controllership, and Master Data teams to improve data quality, reporting efficiency, and process standardisation * Document in\-scope finance processes and maintain up\-to\-date records in the assigned shared location * Support Group\-led initiatives such as system migrations, tool rollouts, or process redesign exercises affecting Content Tech **Strategic Projects** * Provide ad\-hoc financial analysis and modelling to support strategic projects, including M\&A due diligence, acquisition business cases, and divestment assessments * Contribute to the divisional three\-year plan (3YP), building detailed product\-level revenue and cost projections * Support commercial and investment decision\-making with scenario analysis, sensitivity testing, and financial framing for leadership audiences **Skills and Experience** **Essential** * Professional accounting qualification (ACA / ACCA / CIMA or global equivalent) * 3\+ years of post\-qualification experience in an FP\&A or finance business partnering role, with ownership of budget and forecast processes * Experience working within a software or SaaS business, or a multi\-product technology division * Solid understanding of IFRS accounting standards, with practical experience of revenue recognition under IFRS 15 * Strong financial modelling skills and advanced proficiency in Excel * Highly organised with a methodical approach to managing deadlines and competing priorities * Clear and confident communicator, comfortable presenting to senior divisional stakeholders and Group leadership * Experience in preparing management reporting and executive\-level financial packs * Experience driving process automation or finance transformation initiatives * Strong working knowledge of ERP systems and BI/reporting tools * Experience driving process automation or finance transformation initiatives * Experience working with a Shared Service Centre model and Group controllership functions **Beneficial** * Exposure to M\&A activity, due diligence, or strategic planning processes * Leveraging and implementing AI for finance workflows * Conversant in an additional language beyond English Life at RWS: **Life at RWS** **\-** If you like the idea of working with smart people who are passionate about growing the value of ideas, data and content by making sure organizations are understood, then you’ll love life at RWS. Our purpose is to unlock global understanding. This means our work fundamentally recognizes the value of every language and culture. So, we celebrate difference, we are inclusive and believe that diversity makes us strong. We want every employee to grow as an individual and excel in their career. In return, we expect all our people to live by the values that unite us: **to partner**, putting clients fist and winning together**, to pioneer**, innovating fearlessly and leading with vision and courage, **to progress**, aiming high and growing through actions and **to deliver**, owning the outcome and building trust with our colleagues and clients. **RWS embraces DEI** and promotes equal opportunity, we are an Equal Opportunity Employer and prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind. RWS is committed to the principle of equal employment opportunity for all employees and to providing employees with a work environment free of discrimination and harassment. All employment decisions at RWS are based on business needs, job requirements and individual qualifications, without regard to race, religion, nationality, ethnicity, sex, age, disability, or sexual orientation. RWS will not tolerate discrimination based on any of these characteristics. **RWS Values** Get the 3Ps right – Partner, Pioneer, Progress – and we´ll Deliver together as RWS. **Recruitment Agencies:** RWS Holdings PLC does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward any unsolicited resumes to any RWS employees. Any unsolicited resume received will be treated as the property of RWS and Terms \& Conditions associated with the use of such resume will be considered null and void. \#LI\-MS1, \#LI\-Hybrid

title: Finance Intern
description: **Description** At West, we’re a dedicated team that is connected by a purpose to improve patient lives that has been at the center of our Company for more than a century. Our story began when Herman O. West solved the problem of supplying penicillin in mass quantities to the US Government during World War 2\. Through our work to deliver thousands of life\-saving and life\-enhancing injectable medicines to millions of patients daily, West’s indelible mark on the healthcare industry has just begun. A name started our story. How will yours help write our future? There’s no better place to join an inclusive community of professionals with opportunities for lifelong learning, growth and development. Supported by benefit programs, we empower the physical, mental, emotional and financial health of our team members and their families. We believe in giving back to help those in need in the communities where we live and awork. And are equally committed to creating a healthier environment and planet through our sustainability efforts. **Job Summary** To position itself to meet the requirements of continuing rapid business growth and future market development, West Pharmaceutical Services have developed a transformation strategy of standardization, simplification and centralization of global accounting operations. In accordance with that strategy, we are establishing a Global Financial Centre of Excellence in Dublin, Ireland. This internship is an excellent opportunity to develop your skills and gain insights into the finance operations of a dynamic company and exploitation of opportunities offered by new information technologies. **Essential Duties And Responsibilities** * US GAAP Month\-End Close: Support the controllership team in executing the monthly close process, including journal entries, account reconciliations, variance analysis, and preparation of supporting schedules to ensure accurate and timely financial reporting. * Statutory Reporting Platform Implementation (Europe Local GAAP/IFRS): Improvement \& Documentation: Document accounting processes and identify opportunities to enhance efficiency, standardization, automation, and reporting effectiveness within the controllership function. * Ledger \& Financial Operations: Support general ledger maintenance and reconciliations using digital tools and AI\-enabled insights to improve accuracy and control. * Advanced Financial Analysis: Perform in\-depth financial analysis using data analytics techniques to generate actionable insights and develop innovative reporting solutions. * Process Automation \& Digital Enablement: Identify and implement automation opportunities leveraging AI and digital technologies to streamline financial operations. * Project Support: Participate in finance\-related projects assigned by management, applying digital strategies and analytical insights. * Additional Responsibilities: Perform other duties as assigned by the Team Leader or Manager. ****Education**** * Completed a minimum of two years of a Bachelor’s degree program in Accounting, Finance, or a closely related discipline. **Preferred Knowledge, Skills And Abilities** * Proficient in MS Office applications * Team player, positive and flexible attitude * Excellent communication skills i.e. ability to communicate and work effectively with a wide range of stakeholders * Power BI knowledge * The ability to work on their own initiative as part of a hybrid model. * Able to comply with the company’s safety policy at all times. * Able to always comply with the company’s quality policy. **Pay Transparency** The hourly rate for this position is €14\.15\. Total target compensation is determined using objective and gender\-neutral criteria, including relevant experience, qualifications, skills, market benchmarks, geographic location, and the scope and responsibilities of the role.
