title: IT Ops Procurement Technician L1
description: **Company Overview** -------------------- Milestone Technologies is a global IT managed services firm that partners with organizations to scale their technology, infrastructure and services to drive specific business outcomes such as digital transformation, innovation, and operational agility. Milestone is focused on building an employee\-first, performance\-based culture and for over 25 years, we have a demonstrated history of supporting category\-defining enterprise clients that are growing ahead of the market. The company specializes in providing solutions across Application Services and Consulting, Digital Product Engineering, Digital Workplace Services, Private Cloud Services, AI/Automation, and ServiceNow. Milestone culture is built to provide a collaborative, inclusive environment that supports employees and empowers them to reach their full potential. Our seasoned professionals deliver services based on Milestone’s best practices and service delivery framework. By leveraging our vast knowledge base to execute initiatives, we deliver both short\-term and long\-term value to our clients and apply continuous service improvement to deliver transformational benefits to IT. With Intelligent Automation, Milestone helps businesses further accelerate their IT transformation. The result is a sharper focus on business objectives and a dramatic improvement in employee productivity. Through our key technology partnerships and our people\-first approach, Milestone continues to deliver industry\-leading innovation to our clients. With more than 3,000 employees serving over 200 companies worldwide, we are following our mission of revolutionizing the way IT is deployed. **Job Overview** ---------------- ### **Service Desk Technician L2 with A/V Events experience** **(Predominantly MAC/iOS Environment \- 90%)** ### ### **Location****:** **Dublin, Ireland \- Monday through Friday** We’re looking for a Service Desk Technician to join our skilled IT Help Desk Operations team, serving local and global internal users as well as A/V Events. Our goal is to provide excellent technical and non\-technical support with outstanding customer service and timeliness. The Help Desk Technician has experience in supporting both Mac and PC environments, can hold a conversation as well as resolve complex technical issues effectively and efficiently, and is someone who values the people behind the problems. This role works Monday through Friday during standard business hours. **What You Will Need:** * ### **Experience supporting A/V and Events for internal and customer facing events.** * ### **Experience with conference room trouble shooting.** * ### **Familiarity with systems such as Google Meet.** * ### **Minimum 2 years of experience in a Help Desk role w/walk\-up service required, VIP support preferred.** * ### **Experience supporting user requests and performing break/fix or remote installations as needed.** * ### **Demonstrable understanding of Mac OS from command line troubleshooting to resolving individual application failures required.** * ### **Advanced working knowledge of collaboration tools such as Slack, Teams and Google Workspace required** **How You Will Make a Difference:** * ### **Provide hardware and software IT support and technical education to end users both remotely and onsite.** * ### **Applies critical thinking to efficiently troubleshoot simple and complex user requests and issues. and provides as much context and information as possible to deliver the best solutions as \-quickly as possible.** * ### **Troubleshoots client\-side network connectivity issues, including digital authentication, remote access, secure Wi\-Fi, wired connectivity to the internal network, password resets, etc.** * ### **Supports user requests and performs break/fix or remote installations as needed.** * ### **Leads in ticket processes and provides coaching for other Technicians when workflow deficiencies are identified.** * ### **Has deep understanding of defined team metrics, such as backlog, aging, effectiveness; and takes actions based on the current trends in Service.** * ### **Document our systems and ensure our processes are always improving.** * ### **Displays learning agility by actively seeking answers when technically challenged.** * ### **Seeks feedback from other Help Desk Technicians to optimize and improve support, while also maintaining a solid understanding of general user support needs and requirements.** \#LI\-NB2**Compensation** ---------------- Estimated Pay Range: Exact compensation and offers of employment are dependent on circumstances of each case and will be determined based on job\-related knowledge, skills, experience, licenses or certifications, and location. **Our Commitment to Diversity \& Inclusion** -------------------------------------------- *At Milestone we strive to create a workplace that reflects the communities we serve and work with, where we all feel empowered to bring our full, authentic selves to work. We know creating a diverse and inclusive culture that champions equity and belonging is not only the right thing to do for our employees but is also critical to our continued success.* *Milestone Technologies provides equal employment opportunity for all applicants and employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment and will not be discriminated against on the basis of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity, marital status, age, disability, veteran status, sexual orientation, national origin, or any other category protected by applicable federal and state law, or local ordinance. Milestone also makes reasonable accommodations for disabled applicants and employees.* *We welcome the unique background, culture, experiences, knowledge, innovation, self\-expression and perspectives you can bring to our global community. Our recruitment team is looking forward to meeting you.*

title: IT Support Engineer - Contractor (L2)
description: Nostra is a leading Managed Service Provider (MSP) in Ireland, dedicated to delivering advanced IT solutions that enable businesses to build reliable, secure, and scalable IT foundations. With a commitment to continuous improvement, we serve a diverse range of industries, providing exceptional services that empower our clients to focus on their core business goals. This exciting contractor (day rate) position will be based on\-site for initially 12 months with the wider ICT support and Infrastructure team with one of our public sector customers in Dublin City Centre. **Responsibilities:** * Providing end\-user support for PC and peripheral hardware and software issues. * Setting up new hardware for end users. * Installation of application software packages, drivers etc. * Installing and configuring desktop software operating systems and applications. * Deploying PC/Laptop images. Installing software patches. * Record, Update and Track all user issues/requests and correspondence in ticketing system. * Troubleshooting user connectivity and peripheral issues e.g. network problems, including Wi\-Fi connectivity, printing/plotting, password issues, rights issues etc. * Dealing with user issues face to face at the walk\-up Kiosk, when required. * Providing support to the large mobile phone estate. **Requirements:** * Minimum 3 years’ experience in Helpdesk remote support \& onsite support environment. * Strong working knowledge and hands\-on experience in setting up, configuring and managing PCs, notebooks, peripherals (e.g. scanners) in a MS Windows 11 environment. * Expert knowledge of PC imaging technologies. * Strong understanding of Microsoft Active Directory. * Strong working experience with O365\. * Strong working knowledge and hands\-on experience in installing software packages. * Support experience in desktop applications such as email, outlook, browsers, MS office etc. * Strong knowledge and hands\-on experience in troubleshooting network/WIFI connectivity issues including physical networking (e.g. cabling and protocols such as Ethernet and TCP/IP. * Strong working knowledge and hands\-on experience in supporting network printers/plotters in Managed Print Service (MPS) environment. * Working Knowledge of Client based Video Conferencing. * Working knowledge of Baramundi unified endpoint management system or similar. * Strong experience supporting remote users using remote management software such as Dameware/TeamViewer or similar. * Experience supporting remote user Single Sign On and Two Factor Authentication. * Certifications CompTIA A\+, CompTIA Networking\+, MCSA, O365 are a distinct advantage. At Nostra we value our people. We are a passionate team committed to doing our best, our values are trust, accountability, expertise, people, integrity, and empathy. title: IT Support Engineer - Level 2
description: **PFH Technology are currently looking for an experienced L2 IT Support Engineer to join our team in Dublin.** **Objective:** Provide hands on end user support, administration, installation and issue resolution onsite for our client. **Contract:** 12\-month contract (with expected extensions) **Location** : Fully onsite, Dublin 1 **Prerequisites:** ITIL V4, Microsoft Certifications including MD 102 **Skills/Requirements** **1\. Technical Skills** * **Operating Systems:** Advanced knowledge of various operating systems like Windows, macOS, and Linux. * Installing, configuring, and troubleshooting OS\-related issues. * Knowledge of Active Directory (AD), Group Policies, and domain\-related issues (for Windows environments). * **Network Troubleshooting:** * In\-depth understanding of networking concepts such as DNS, DHCP, TCP/IP, subnetting, VLANs, and firewalls. * Diagnosing and resolving issues related to routers, switches, firewalls, and VPNs. * Monitoring and maintaining network performance. * **Hardware and Software Troubleshooting:** * Ability to diagnose hardware failures (laptops, desktops, servers). * Knowledge of software deployment, updates, and patch management. * **Virtualization:** Familiarity with virtualization platforms like VMware, Hyper\-V, or VirtualBox. * **Cloud Technologies:** Understanding cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) and managing cloud services like virtual machines, storage, and cloud backups. * **Scripting and Automation:** Basic scripting knowledge (PowerShell, Bash, Python) for automating routine tasks and troubleshooting complex issues. **2\. Advanced Troubleshooting and Problem Solving** * **Root Cause Analysis:** Ability to perform deep dives into issues to identify root causes rather than simply resolving symptoms. * **Log Analysis:** Reading and interpreting system and network logs to diagnose and resolve technical issues. * **System Performance Tuning:** Diagnosing performance bottlenecks in hardware, applications, and networks, and suggesting improvements. * **Incident Management:** Following standard ITIL (Information Technology Infrastructure Library) practices for incident escalation, documentation, and resolution. **3\. Communication Skills** * **Customer Service:** Communicating complex technical information in a user\-friendly way to non\-technical staff or clients. * **Collaboration:** Working effectively with Level 1 (frontline) support teams, and escalating issues to Level 3 or specialized support teams when necessary. * **Documentation:** Creating and updating technical documentation, including knowledge base articles and standard operating procedures (SOPs). * **Ticket Management:** Using ticketing systems (e.g., Jira, ServiceNow, Zendesk) to manage and resolve technical support requests. **4\. Security Awareness** * **Cybersecurity Knowledge:** Awareness of security best practices, threat mitigation, and basic concepts like encryption, VPNs, multi\-factor authentication (MFA), and firewalls. * **Endpoint Security:** Ensuring that workstations, laptops, and mobile devices are secured against potential threats. * **Backup and Disaster Recovery:** Familiarity with backup solutions, disaster recovery planning, and ensuring data integrity. **5\. Database Management** * **Basic SQL Knowledge:** Understanding of databases and SQL queries for troubleshooting database\-related issues or working with database administrators (DBAs). * **Backup and Restore:** Ability to perform basic database backups and restore operations in case of failure. **6\. Project Support** * **Support for IT Projects:** Participating in IT projects such as software rollouts, infrastructure upgrades, or migrations. * **Change Management:** Understanding change control processes and ensuring that system changes are well\-documented and implemented according to organizational policies. **7\. Remote Support Tools** * **Remote Troubleshooting:** Proficiency with tools like TeamViewer, AnyDesk, or Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) for assisting users remotely. * **Monitoring Tools:** Familiarity with network and system monitoring tools (e.g., Nagios, SolarWinds, PRTG) to proactively identify issues. **8\. Time Management and Prioritization** * **Task Prioritization:** Ability to prioritize tasks based on the severity and impact of technical issues. * **Multitasking:** Managing multiple incidents or service requests at the same time while ensuring timely resolution. **9\. Vendor and Third\-Party Support** * **Vendor Management:** Liaising with third\-party vendors for escalated technical issues related to specific hardware or software products. * **Warranty and RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization) Management:** Handling warranty claims and equipment replacements through vendor support. **10\. Certifications:** **Essential** * **Microsoft Certifications:** (e.g., Microsoft Certified: Azure Administrator, Microsoft 365 Certified). * **ITIL Foundation V4:** For understanding IT service management (ITSM) processes. **Optional but Beneficial** * **CompTIA Network\+, CompTIA Security\+** for networking and security fundamentals. * **Cisco Certifications:** (e.g., CCNA for networking fundamentals). **Education** * Third Level Education Qualification is preferable. PFH Technology, owned by Ricoh – a Japanese based global ICT company, is a premier provider of end\-to\-end ICT solutions and a managed services portfolio scaling from SMEs to large Enterprise organisations. We have unrivalled vendor relationships. We can procure, design, deploy and support all your ICT needs. Our ISO certified Custodian™ Cloud Services and Custodian™ Managed Services provide the technology and expertise to mitigate risk and reduce your costs immediately. We have a nationwide network of over 750 dedicated professionals, ready to meet your ICT needs, with offices in Dublin, Cork and Galway.

title: Junior Chef
description: Job Introduction * Permanent Part time * 24 hours per week * 8:00am \- 2:30pm * €15 per hour * Free Onsite Parking * Monday to Thursday Working Pattern * A culture that values teamwork, creativity, hospitality, and continuous improvement **Junior Chef with The Good Food Company** **LittleConnell Newbridge \- Part time** ***A recipe for success; Serve incredible food, enjoy incredible perks.*** **At The Good Food Company**, you'll belong in a team and thrive in service. We know that our talented chefs are the key to delivering exceptional service and tasteful experiences for our clients, customers, and employees. We are looking for a reliable and enthusiastic Kitchen Assistant / Junior Chef to join our busy workplace hospitality operation. It's the perfect balance between a rewarding kitchen career and quality time outside of work.** **A little more about The Good Food Company:** * Monday to Thursday working pattern * Daytime hours only – no evenings or weekends * 20 days annual leave/year plus bank holidays off * Great coffee and delicious meals at work * Referral Scheme and Cycle to Work Scheme * Supportive, inclusive, and people\-focused team culture * Training, development, and opportunities for progression * Work with fresh food and quality ingredients * Access to a high\-quality workplace hospitality operation * A culture that values teamwork, creativity, hospitality, and continuous improvement If you're someone who enjoys working with fresh food, takes pride in delivering quality, and wants to be part of a team that genuinely cares about what it does, we'd love to hear from you. About Us At The Good Food Company, we strive every day to set a new standard for corporate dining by providing an inspiring, delicious, farm\-to\-fork dining experience that extends beyond the plate. The Good Food Company was cooked up with passion around a kitchen table in the UK in 1999\. We started with one big dream — to bring restaurant\-quality food to corporate dining — and have been championing flavour and food creativity ever since. * The Good Food Company wholeheartedly supports the principle of equal opportunities in employment and opposes all forms of unlawful or unfair discrimination on the grounds of age, disability, religion or belief, colour, nationality, ethnic or national origin, race, gender, marital status or sexual orientation.

title: Junior Product Designer
description: **Ireland based only.** **Please note: We are not engaging recruitment agencies for this position.** **Important: This role does not meet the requirements for work permit application.** **The Product Design Team** The Product Design team is a collaborative group of designers and researchers working across multiple locations, partnering closely with Product, Engineering, Data, and Commercial teams to create player\-centred experiences at scale. The team is passionate about delivering high\-quality, data\-informed design solutions that balance user needs with business objectives. A strong focus is placed on craft, clarity of thinking, continuous improvement, and measurable impact. The team embraces modern tools and AI\-enabled workflows to work more effectively, enhance collaboration, and drive innovation across the product experience. **What you will be doing:** **Craft \& Execution** * Deliver clear, usable, and accessible design work across web and mobile platforms. * Create wireframes, flows, and high fidelity designs using Figma. * Use the Gaming design system and contribute suggestions for improvement. * Iterate on designs based on feedback, data, and research insights. **Product Thinking** * Work with Product Managers and Engineers to understand user problems and business goals. * Break down problem spaces into clear and testable solutions. * Develop an understanding of how user needs, technical constraints, and commercial goals intersect. **Collaboration \& Communication** * Collaborate closely with developers, product managers, and stakeholders throughout the design and delivery process. * Participate in critiques and team rituals, seeking feedback and contributing ideas. * Support clear documentation and handover to ensure design intent is delivered in build. **Leadership \& Influence** * Take ownership of your work by bringing clarity, asking thoughtful questions, and following through on delivery. * Demonstrate a willingness to learn from others and contribute to a supportive team environment. **Strategic Impact** * Contribute design work that supports both player satisfaction and business performance. * Develop an understanding of how your work aligns with team and company goals. **Inclusive Design \& Team Culture** * Embed accessibility and inclusive design practices into your work. * Contribute positively to team culture through collaboration, openness, and participation in team rituals. **Design Systems** * Work within the design system and follow established patterns and standards. * Suggest improvements and learn how systems evolve over time. **Customer Research** * Support research activities such as usability testing and insight gathering. * Demonstrate a natural curiosity about our players, seeking to deeply understand their needs, motivations, behaviours, and pain points. * Apply research findings to improve your design solutions, with a focus on influencing positive human behavioural change and delivering meaningful player outcomes. **Who we are looking for:** * 1 to 3 years of experience designing digital products, or equivalent experience through internships, education, or strong portfolio work. * A portfolio that demonstrates structured thinking, problem solving, and attention to detail. * Comfortable using Figma and familiar with design systems. * Strong collaboration skills and a growth mindset. * Able to manage your time across multiple tasks with guidance and support. * Interested in gaming, entertainment products, or complex consumer platforms. * Demonstrates curiosity, initiative, and a growth mindset. * Collaborates effectively with others and contributes positively to team culture. * User\-focused, detail\-oriented, and open to feedback and continuous improvement. **What we offer:** * Competitive salary * Company pension * Discretionary annual bonus depending on your performance and the company's performance * Tax Saver Commuter scheme * Bike to work scheme * Comprehensive health insurance * Income Protection Plan * Life Assurance * e\-learning platforms (plenty of resources for your development) * Referral Programme * Flexitime * 24\-day annual leave that increases with length of service * Birthday Leave (1 extra day off to celebrate your day) **More about evoke** We’re a business that embraces change and progress. The power behind big name brands William Hill, 888 and Mr Green, **evoke** is the new name for 888 Holdings. Marking a new sense of purpose, direction and ambition for the business, there couldn’t be a more exciting time to join us as we accelerate our journey to bring even greater delight to our customers with world\-class betting and gaming experiences. That’s the future. That’s **evoke** . At **evoke** , you’ll benefit from flexibility and a culture built on trust. We’ll give you the space to be yourself and the tools you need to protect our customers while they play. We’ll invest in your future to help you develop your unique strengths and build a career that’s right for you. **Apply** At **evoke** , we prioritize diversity, equity, and inclusion for the benefit of our company, employees, and communities. We foster a welcoming and safe workplace that values all forms of diversity and provides opportunities for growth. Sound good? Then you belong at our place! The first step in the recruitment process is kickstarting your application, followed by an initial screening call and an interview stage. **Apply today to kickstart your application with the evoke Family!**

title: Junior Sales Specialist - Fintech
description: We are a rapidly growing software provider and progressive business that offers a variety of digital banking solutions for its customers and organisations all over the world. We are now looking for a **Junior Sales Specialist** to join our expanding sales function, supporting different product verticals within the Financial Services ecosystem. **Responsibilities:** * Develop a strong understanding of sales trends and developments within the Financial Services and Payments industry. * Establish, develop, and maintain positive business relationships with prospective and existing clients. * Present, promote, and sell products and services using structured and persuasive sales approaches. * Actively reach out to customer leads through various channels. * Manage and resolve client queries and issues to maximise satisfaction. * Achieve agreed sales targets and outcomes within set timelines. * Coordinate sales activities with internal teams and relevant departments. * Provide regular reports on customer needs, market feedback, competitive activity, and sales performance. * Support the preparation of sales materials and PowerPoint presentations. * Organise and schedule meetings in line with business priorities. **Team \& Product Focus:** Depending on business needs, the role may support different sales teams, with a focus on one of the following areas: * Selling multiple payment solutions, including at least five different payment methods (e.g. cards, local payment methods, alternative payments, digital wallets, etc.), or * Selling credit card products, with the ability to manage and close sales for a minimum of five card\-based solutions **Requirements:** * Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Sales, or a related field. * Experience or strong interest in Finance, Fintech, or Payment industries. * Understanding of sales processes and customer acquisition fundamentals. * Excellent communication, presentation, and negotiation skills. * Strong time management and organisational abilities. * Analytical mindset with problem\-solving skills. * Ability to work independently while collaborating effectively with wider teams.

title: Junior Security Analyst, Threat Detection Operations
description: ### **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience. * 2 years of experience with security assessments or security design reviews or threat modeling. * Experience with host, network, and cloud security principles and concepts (e.g., common attacker tactics, tools, and techniques (TTPs)). ### **Preferred qualifications:** * Certification in Security\+ or similar Cyber Security/Incident Response. * Experience analyzing, triaging, and remediating common information security incidents. * Experience with automation via coding. * Ability to work separately, prioritize, and multitask. * Excellent problem\-solving, investigative, and written and verbal communication skills. * Excellent log/data analysis skill analysing a large set of data using sql or similar query language. **About the job** ----------------- In this role, you will develop and maintain the signals, tools, and infrastructure that help Google evolve to match attackers. You will conduct and guide others in security analysis, risk hunting, and malware and indicator analysis. You will manage malicious activity on Google's networks. You will perform analysis of risks on enterprise, production, and cloud environments.The Core team builds the technical foundation behind Google’s flagship products. We are owners and advocates for the underlying design elements, developer platforms, product components, and infrastructure at Google. These are the essential building blocks for excellent, safe, and coherent experiences for our users and drive the pace of innovation for every developer. We look across Google’s products to build central solutions, break down technical barriers and strengthen existing systems. As the Core team, we have a mandate and a unique opportunity to impact important technical decisions across the company.Individual pay is determined by factors including job\-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Ireland: €68000 \- €70000 (EUR) \+ 15% bonus target \+ equity \+ benefits Learn more about benefits at Google.**Responsibilities** -------------------- * Analyze, investigate, and respond to security alerts. * Research new risk hunting techniques to identify/discover security risks. * Automate workflows and develop solutions to reduce operational toil. * Ensure compliance with legal mandates and internal security and privacy policies. * Manage response efforts for low severity incidents. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form.

title: Lab IT Administrator (Dublin / Swords)
description: ALGOTEQUE is an IT consultancy firm that helps startups, mid\-sized and large corporations to create and deliver innovative technologies. Our team has a successful track record in designing, developing, implementing, and integrating software solutions (AI, ML, BI, Web, Automation) for Telecom, Energy, Bank, Insurance, Pharma, Automotive, Industry, e\-commerce. We deliver our services both in fixed\-price and time\-and\-materials models, helping our customers achieve their business and IT strategies. **Job Description** We are looking for a Lab IT Administrator to support GxP\-controlled lab IT systems, ensuring reliable operations, regulatory compliance, and end\-user support in a pharmaceutical environment. Location: Ireland \- Dublin / Swords. **Responsibilities** * Provide end\-user support and administration for lab equipment, servers, desktops, and software. * Troubleshoot issues across software, hardware, networks, and scientific instruments. * Perform system monitoring, maintenance, backups, and user access management. * Author and maintain CSV documentation (URS, FS/CS, IQ/OQ/PQ, test scripts, SOPs). * Ensure compliance with 21 CFR Part 11, EU Annex 11, and Data Integrity standards. * Collaborate with CSV Lead, System Integrators, Site IT, and Operations. * Act as SME for lab IT systems, supporting audits and inspections. **Profile / Requirements** * Bachelor's degree (or higher) in a technical discipline. * Professional experience in IT / digital roles. * Strong communication skills (written and verbal). * Ability to multitask and manage competing priorities. * Proven experience identifying operational issues and implementing effective solutions. **Nice To Have** * Experience in a GxP\-regulated environment. * Hands\-on experience with Pharmaceutical QC Lab standalone COTS systems. * End\-to\-end experience with CSV lifecycle documentation, including system use and administration procedures. * Solid understanding of Data Integrity principles. * Experience supporting audits and regulatory inspections. AO3980

title: Laboratory Analyst in Forensic Science, PUBLICJOBS
description: **Title:** Laboratory Analyst in Forensic Science Ireland Forensic Science Ireland (FSI) is an associated office of the Department of Justice, Home Affairs \& Migration and plays a pivotal role in Ireland’s criminal justice system. Its mission is to provide the Irish Justice System with a high\-quality integrated Forensic Service through independent, expert scientific analysis and advice to best international standards. FSI now has vacancies for Laboratory Analysts. This is an entry level grade and offers a great opportunity for scientific and technical professionals as well as those embarking on their professional careers. Successful candidates will become part of a dynamic and growing professional STEM\-based organisation in a progressive field that is critical for Ireland’s criminal justice system. Reporting to a Senior Laboratory Analyst, Laboratory Analysts will be involved in delivering on a range of analytical techniques and generating reliable results on an on\-going basis. Successful candidates will also help develop new techniques and processes to ensure that FSI is at the forefront of forensic technology and practice. Full details of the role, including specific eligibility requirements, is available on www.publicjobs.ie. The closing date for receipt of completed applications is **3pm on Thursday 2nd of July 2026** . *We are committed to a policy of equal opportunity and encourage applications under all nine grounds of the Employment Equality Act.*

title: MHPSS Cultural Mediation Associate (Georgian)
description: Applications are welcome from internal and external candidates. For all IOM vacancies, applications from qualified and eligible first\-tier candidates are considered before those of qualified and eligible second\-tier candidates in the selection process. For the purpose of this vacancy, internal candidates are considered first\-tier candidates. Context IOM Ireland has been operating in Ireland since 2001, currently in following areas of migration management: Assisted Voluntary Return and Reintegration, Resettlement, Family Reunification, Counter\-Trafficking, Protection, Integration Support to Beneficiaries of Temporary Protection, Integrated Immigration Governance and Mental Health and Psychosocial Support (MHPSS). IOM provides mental health and psychosocial support to international protection seekers residing in the Citywest accommodation facility with the aim to improve their psychosocial well\-being and to strengthen their overall resilience. Under the overall supervision of the Chief of Mission in Ireland, the Head of MHPSS Unit, the Cultural Mediation and Community Engagement Coordinator and direct supervision of Senior Cultural Mediation Associate, the incumbent will support the implementation of activities related to community mobilization and pertinent activities for international protection applicants hosted in Citywest accommodation centre. * RESPONSIBILITIES Provide cultural mediation and interpretation support by facilitating communication between service providers and migrants (from English into different migrants’ mother tongue and vice versa), while always respecting the principles of neutrality, accuracy, impartiality, confidentiality. * Provide verbal interpretation support to the International Protection Accommodation Services (IPAS) staff in Citywest to support day\-to\-day communication with beneficiaries from English into different migrants’ languages. * Assist through interpretation during the collection of routine information and data and case identification and referrals. * Support the beneficiaries in contacting service providers within Citywest by providing interpretation and mediation support in an impartial and neutral manner. * Support the identification of vulnerabilities from the initial reception into the centre and throughout the continued communication with beneficiaries and refer them to the Senior Case Management Associate for case management. * Support IOM partners in the implementation of community engagement and awareness\-raising activities by assisting with logistics, interpretation, and beneficiary outreach. * Conduct outreach activities to inform beneficiaries about available MHPSS services and scheduled activities, in coordination with MHPSS team. * Provide interpretation assistance to mental health professionals during scheduled group and individual counselling sessions, focus group discussions and psycho\-education activities, including remote interpretation (phone or online), when required. * Prepare routine documentation related to the project and submit basic reports to the Project Management Team on activities performed in Citywest. * Any other related task, as required. QUALIFICATIONS Education * High school diploma with five years of relevant experience; or, * Bachelor’s degree in Communication, Social Sciences, International relations or related fields from an accredited academic institution with at least 3 years of relevant professional experience. Accredited Universities are those listed in the UNESCO World Higher Education Database . Experience * Completion of trainings in cultural mediation for mental health settings, and competence in the MHPSS specialized vocabulary would be an asset. * Experience in cultural mediation in the framework of migrants’ assistance. * Previous experience with international organizations, NGOs, EU Agencies and/or Irish law enforcements or Government’s Departments would be an asset. Skills * Ability to work with migrants and asylum seekers. * Interpersonal skills (i.e. empathy and emotional resilience) * Intercultural competence and communication skills. * Culture\-specific knowledge. * Capacity to prevent and mitigate misunderstandings due to language barriers by facilitating communication in an objective and impartial manner. * Basic understanding in interpersonal communication and sensitivity in addressing the asylum\-seekers’ claims. * Knowledge of the procedures related to the asylum application in Ireland. * Ability to prepare clear and concise routine reports and basic written summaries. * Good working knowledge of Microsoft Office. Languages * For this position, fluency in English is required (oral and written). * Fluency or mother tongue in Georgian is required IOM’s official languages are English, French and Spanish. Proficiency of language(s) required will be specifically evaluated during the selection process, which may include written and/or oral assessments. **Required Competencies** Competencies will be assessed during the selection process. Values \- all IOM staff members must abide by and demonstrate these three values: * Inclusion and respect for diversity: Respects and promotes individual and cultural differences. Encourages diversity and inclusion. * Integrity and transparency: Maintains high ethical standards and acts in a manner consistent with organizational principles/rules and standards of conduct. * Professionalism: Demonstrates ability to work in a composed, competent and committed manner and exercises careful judgment in meeting day\-to\-day challenges. * Courage: Demonstrates willingness to take a stand on issues of importance. * Empathy: Shows compassion for others, makes people feel safe, respected and fairly treated. Core Competencies – behavioural indicators (Level 1\) * Teamwork: Develops and promotes effective collaboration within and across units to achieve shared goals and optimize results. * Delivering results: Produces and delivers quality results in a service\-oriented and timely manner. Is action oriented and committed to achieving agreed outcomes. * Managing and sharing knowledge: Continuously seeks to learn, share knowledge and innovate. * Accountability: Takes ownership for achieving the Organization’s priorities and assumes responsibility for own actions and delegated work. * Communication: Encourages and contributes to clear and open communication. Explains complex matters in an informative, inspiring and motivational way. Managerial Competencies – behavioural indicators (Level 1\) (applicable only if position is with direct reports) * Leadership: Provides a clear sense of direction, leads by example and demonstrates the ability to carry out the Organization’s vision. Assists others to realize and develop their leadership and professional potential. * Empowering others: Creates an enabling environment where staff can contribute their best and develop their potential. * Building Trust: Promotes shared values and creates an atmosphere of trust and honesty. * Strategic thinking and vision: Works strategically to realize the Organization’s goals and communicates a clear strategic direction. * Humility: Leads with humility and shows openness to acknowledging own shortcomings. **Notes*** Any offer made to the candidate in relation to this vacancy notice is subject to funding confirmation. * This selection process may be used to staff similar positions in various duty stations. Recommended candidates will remain eligible to be appointed in a similar position for a period of 24 months. * This post is subject to local recruitment. Only those holding a valid residence and work permit for the country where this position is based will be eligible for consideration. * Appointment will be subject to certification that the candidate is medically fit for appointment, verification of residency, visa, and authorizations by the concerned Government, where applicable. * IOM has a zero\-tolerance policy on conduct that is incompatible with the aims and objectives of the United Nations and IOM, including sexual exploitation and abuse, sexual harassment, abuse of authority and discrimination based on gender, nationality, age, race, sexual orientation, religious or ethnic background or disabilities. * IOM does not charge a fee at any stage of its recruitment process (application, interview, processing, training or other fee). IOM does not request any information related to bank accounts. * IOM only accepts duly completed applications submitted through the IOM online recruitment system. The online tool also allows candidates to track the status of their application. * A s the right to live and work in Ireland is required, the contract issued may be shorter than the period mentioned in the advertisement, in order to align with expiry date of the work permit. For further information and other job postings, you are welcome to visit our website: IOM Careers and Job Vacancies

title: Management Trainee
description: Location: Naas, Co. Kildare Job Type: Full\-time, Permanent SuperValu Naas is looking for a motivated and enthusiastic Trainee Manager to join our team. This is an excellent opportunity for someone who is passionate about retail, customer service, and team leadership, and who wants to build a long\-term career in supermarket management. About the Role As a Trainee Manager, you will work closely with senior management to learn all aspects of running a busy supermarket. You will gain hands\-on experience across store operations, customer service, team supervision, stock control, and sales performance. This role is ideal for someone who is eager to learn, takes initiative, and thrives in a fast\-paced retail environment. Key Responsibilities * Support the day\-to\-day running of the store and ensure high standards are maintained at all times. * Deliver excellent customer service and help create a positive shopping experience. * Assist with supervising, motivating, and developing team members. * Help manage stock levels, merchandising, and product availability. * Ensure the store is clean, safe, and well\-presented. * Support management with sales targets, promotions, and operational goals. * Learn and apply company procedures, policies, and retail best practices. * Assist in dealing with customer queries and resolving issues professionally. Candidate Requirements * Previous experience in retail, customer service, or a supervisory role is an advantage. * Strong communication and interpersonal skills. * A positive attitude and willingness to learn. * Good organisational skills and the ability to work in a busy environment. * Leadership potential and a strong work ethic. * Flexibility to work across different shifts, including weekends. What We Offer * Full training and development * Opportunity to progress into a management role * Supportive team environment * Hands\-on retail management experience * Competitive pay, depending on experience How to Apply If you are ambitious, hardworking, and ready to start your career in retail management, we would love to hear from you. Apply today with your CV to hrrushegroup@supervalu.ie Pay: €35,000\.00\-€35,500\.00 per year Ability to commute/relocate: * Naas, County Kildare: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Experience: * retail : 1 year (preferred) Language: * english (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Mechanical Design Engineer
description: Elusav Recruitment have an excellent opportunity for a Projects Delivery Engineer for a leading Engineering and Technical Services Contractor in the water and wastewater treatment space. They are looking for a Projects Delivery Engineer who can support and manage a diverse range of design projects, both greenfield and brownfield in nature, as well as support preconstruction/proposal activities. This role sits within the companies contracting division, but they also operate large managed services contracts for Ireland largest WWTP facilities, where majority of the projects are delivered. The Projects Delivery Engineer will report to the Senior Projects managers and support the delivery of critical projects up to the value of €40 Million. Excellent opportunity to join an industry leader with a significant footprint in the wastewater treatment space in Ireland. Long term local opportunity, and also strong remuneration package. **Responsibilities:** * Production of documentation, including reports, drawings, calculations, specifications, tender documentation, and particular specifications. * Production of project budgets and resource\-loaded detailed work programmes. * Co\-ordinating with PSCS, PSDP, Client, Operations and other stakeholders. * Providing technical advice and guidance. * Providing input to bids and project managing appropriate projects. * Supervision \& management of contractors. * Responding to Client requests for information. * Preparation and/or review RAMS. * Identify interfaces with operations, impacts, consequences, work\-arounds and plan, execute operations dependent tasks within the suitable window. * Support maintenance by managing SLAs and contractors. * Identify and prioritise capital maintenance projects based on equipment/process condition and criticality. **Requirements:** * Good knowledge of techniques for planning, monitoring and controlling projects. * Ability to risk assess equipment and criticality * Have experience in design at all levels, from feasibility to a "for construction" level. * Have considerable technical knowledge of all aspects of design as it impacts technical, budgetary and programme issues. * PRINCE2 or PMP qualification or experience in a PRINCE2/PMP structured project is preferred. **Contact:** If you would like to learn more about the vacancy, apply now, or contact Brendan Mullins at bmullins@elusav.com or \+353 61 527 150\.

title: Move to B2B Sales - Open to All Backgrounds
description: 1. **Full training from day one.** 2. **Tailored for You:** One\-to\-one coaching built around your strengths and growth areas 3. **Learn while you work** – Structured, hands\-on training with daily support. Access is growing and hiring motivated **professionals from all backgrounds. We don’t expect experience \- we provide it.** Through structured training and ongoing mentorship, we develop confident **Business Developers** and future leaders. **What You’ll Get** * **structured training from day one:** 150\+ learning modules, hands\-on experience, and expert\-led coaching give you a clear framework to build your sales skills and confidence. * **Mentorship that drives growth:** Daily 1:1 coaching, ongoing feedback, and industry mentors support your development while encouraging ownership of your progress. * **Performance\-based career progression:** A transparent promotion pathway with internal promotions only means every leader has advanced from the same starting point, with many earning their first promotion within 6 months. * **Continuous development into leadership:** Ongoing training, recognised certifications, and leadership pathways help you grow from entry level into senior business development and management roles. **What You’ll Do** As a **Business Developer** , you will be responsible for the **entire sales journey** , from first contact to signed agreement. More simply, you will: * Finding and understanding potential clients * Starting conversations that turn into real commercial discussions * Managing the full sales process through to close * Building long\-term client relationships **Additional Benefits** * **Epic Company Trips:** all\-inclusive European trips celebrating team success * From breakfast together in the morning to the fully stocked beer fridge on Fridays * **Personalised benefits package:** health insurance, gym membership, and more * **Social and wellness events:** stay connected, supported, and part of a strong community * Average earnings: **€45k\+ in Year 1** , with strong growth trajectory into Year 2 to **€65k\+** **Sound Like Your Next Move?** For more information, contact Nadia Garcia on \+353 1 572 1159 or email your CV to nadia.garcia@accesshealthcare.ie.

title: Multi Skilled Carpenter - Residential Projects - Full Time
description: Multi Skilled Carpenter \- Residential Renovations (Full Time Work) Location: North Dublin, Ireland Employment Type: Full\-Time Salary: €30 \- €37 hour Working Hours: 8\-5am M\-T; 8\-4pm Friday **About Us** Deeley Construction is a well\-established company specializing in high\-end residential renovations and extensions. Our projects demand precision, initiative, and an ability to take ownership of the work—from breaking ground to final handover. We pride ourselves on our commitment to quality, professionalism, and maintaining a safe, tidy, and respectful work environment. **Role Overview** We are seeking a highly experienced and self\-motivated Multi Skilled Carpenter to support our residential construction projects from start to finish. This is a hands\-on role ideal for someone who brings broad construction experience, strong problem\-solving ability, and a proactive mindset. You must be capable of working independently, making sound decisions on site, and contributing across all phases of the build \- from groundworks to final completion. You’ll be expected to think on your feet, solve problems without fuss, and keep progress moving without needing to be micromanaged. **Key Responsibilities** * Undertake a wide range of construction tasks including demolition, groundworks, steelwork, roofing, structural work, and internal and external finishing. * Own the progress of your tasks, using initiative and practical knowledge to identify solutions and keep momentum on site. * Apply common sense and site experience to get the job done safely and to a high standard \- even when plans evolve. * Read and interpret technical drawings, blueprints, and specifications with precision. * Maintain a clean, organized, and hazard\-free work environment. * Ensure correct and safe use of all tools and machinery. * Liaise with supervisors and colleagues as needed to ensure smooth site operations. * Uphold high standards of workmanship and site conduct at all times. **Requirements** * Minimum 10 years’ experience in residential construction and renovation. * Demonstrated ability to work independently and solve problems on\-site. * Strong experience across key phases of construction including groundworks, structural work, and roofing. * Own tools and reliable transport (must be able to bring tools to site daily). * Safe Pass and Manual Handling certification preferred (or willingness to obtain). * In\-depth knowledge of Irish building codes and site health and safety. * Excellent timekeeping, reliability, and communication skills. * Strong physical fitness and ability to handle the demands of on\-site work. * Strong site initiative \- you understand how a build flows, where problems may arise, and how to handle them calmly and competently. * A reputation for being dependable, tidy, and easy to work with. **Desirable Skills** * Ability to operate small machinery (e.g. excavators, teleporters) is a plus. * Experience with tiling, plaster patching, and remedial works. * Experience setting out small works and ensuring levels and measurements are correct. * Flexible, “can\-do” attitude with a proactive, problem\-solving mindset. **What We Offer** * Competitive salary based on experience and skillset. * A supportive and respectful work environment. * High\-quality residential projects where skilled craftsmanship is valued. * Opportunities for professional growth and development. * A strong team culture focused on safety, quality, and trust. If you’re a highly experienced construction operative who thrives in a role with autonomy, variety, and accountability, this is the position for you. To Apply: Please send your CV and a short cover letter outlining your experience and suitability for the role. References will be requested at interview stage. Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €30\.00\-€37\.00 per hour Benefits: * Company events Application question(s): * Do you own your own set of tools? * Do you have your own transport? Experience: * Residential Carpentry : 10 years (preferred) Language: * Fluent english (required) Licence/Certification: * A Driving Licence (required) Location: * North Dublin, County Dublin (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: Network Engineer
description: **Job title: Network Engineer (Mergers \& Acquisitions team)** **Job Location: Baldonnell Lower, Dublin 22, Ireland** **Mode of hire: Contract 1 year with rolling (Citizen only)** **Mode of work: Work from Office** **Key Responsibilities** * **Design and Implementation:** Design and deploy new network solutions (LAN/WAN, VPNs, wireless, cloud networking, SDWAN/MPLS) and/or improve the efficiency of existing networks. (Including building of detailed/accurate bill of materials for design components). * **Configuration and Maintenance:** Install, configure, and support network equipment, including routers, switches, firewalls, load balancers, proxy servers, and WAPs. * **Performance Monitoring and Troubleshooting:** Monitor network performance, conduct data analysis, troubleshoot outages and complex connectivity issues, and schedule upgrades and patches to maximize uptime. * **Security Management:** Some previous experience with network security measures, including firewalls, intrusion detection/prevention systems (IDS/IPS) and access controls, preferred. * **Documentation and Compliance:** Create and maintain detailed documentation, diagrams, and configuration logs, and ensure all network operations comply with security standards and best practices. * **Collaboration and Support:** Provide technical support to staff, liaise with vendors and service providers, and collaborate with other IT teams (security, systems, application) on various projects. * **Capacity Planning:** Perform capacity management and planning to ensure the network infrastructure can scale with the organization's growth. **Required Qualifications** * **Education:** Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, Network Engineering, or a related field. * **Experience:** Proven hands\-on network engineering / administration experience, 15\+ years * **Certifications (Desired):** * **Cisco:** CCNA, CCNP, or CCIE * **General:** CompTIA Network\+ or Security\+ * **Cloud:** Cloud certifications beneficial (CCSK and/or an AWS certification) **Technical Skills** * Deep understanding of networking protocols (TCP/IP, BGP, OSPF, DNS, DHCP, MPLS, IPsec). * Hands\-on experience with enterprise\-grade equipment from vendors like Cisco, Aruba, and Palo Alto. * Proficiency with network monitoring and diagnostic tools (e.g., Wireshark, SolarWinds). * Knowledge of cloud networking and virtualization technologies. * Familiarity with scripting and automation tools/languages (e.g., Python, Bash) is increasingly beneficial. * Ability to follow precise company standards in design and configuration of new networks. **Soft Skills** * Strong analytical and complex problem\-solving abilities. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills to explain technical issues to non\-technical stakeholders and also to clearly communicate with people with varying skills in English. * Time management and project management skills to prioritize tasks and meet deadlines. * Ability to work independently and as part of a collaborative team. * Excellent attention to small details, both technical and logistical and differences between all global locations. * Ability to work globally, sensitively and across many cultures and countries.

title: Network Engineer - Contractor (L2)
description: Nostra is a leading Managed Service Provider (MSP) in Ireland, dedicated to delivering advanced IT solutions that enable businesses to build reliable, secure, and scalable IT foundations. With a commitment to continuous improvement, we serve a diverse range of industries, providing exceptional services that empower our clients to focus on their core business goals. This exciting contractor (day rate) position will be based for 12 months on\-site with the wider ICT support and Infrastructure team with one of our public sector customers in Dublin City Centre. You will be responsible for managing and supporting a large, complex WAN environment across multiple locations throughout Ireland, delivered via a range of service providers. The network spans a multi\-vendor infrastructure, including FortiGate SD\-WAN firewalls, Alcatel\-Lucent Omni Switches, and Aruba WiFi solutions. Connectivity across the estate is diverse, incorporating technologies such as MPLS, eFibre, DSL, MSA, 4G/5G, and satellite links. SD\-WAN, primarily based on Fortinet technology, is deployed extensively across the network estate. **Key Responsibilities:** **Project Delivery, Documentation and Reporting:** * Understand the goals of collaborate effectively with internal teams. * Plan, coordinate and track project tasks proactively. * Contribute to project reviews with team. * Maintain detailed and up\-to\-date documentation of network diagrams, configurations, and procedures. * Update the inventory management system. **Network Maintenance and Support:** * Monitor and maintain LAN, WAN and WLAN infrastructure, including switches, routers, access points, SD\-WAN Firewalls and NAC. * Troubleshoot and resolve network issues to minimise downtime. **On\-site Physical Work:** * Cabling: Inspect, install, and maintain network cabling for connectivity and performance. * Racking and Stacking: Install and organise network equipment in racks with proper cable management. **Network Implementation:** * Assist in deploying LAN, WLAN, and NAC solutions as required. * Support Level 3/4 engineers in configuring and installing network hardware and software as required * Facilitate planned maintenance and change management processes. **Support:** * Offer technical support and guidance to the clients users and help desk * Respond to Level 2 and priority helpdesk tickets as required * Record, Update, Track issues/requests and correspondence in Helpdesk ticketing system **Working Conditions:** * Onsite role at clients location. * Standard working hours of 37\.5 hours per week. * May involve occasional after\-hours work to assist in maintenance or upgrades. * Requires the ability to lift and move network equipment when necessary. * Travel at various sites across Ireland will be required at times. **Requirements:** * 2\+ years working experience managing and supporting Network environments. * Full driving license and access to vehicle. * Flexibility to travel will be required across Ireland. * Hands\-on experience in setting up and supporting Network infrastructure. * Hands\-on experience in setting up or managing Wireless networks. * Working knowledge of LAN/WAN protocols such as TCP/IP, DNS/DHCP, Layer2, Switching, Layer3 Routing, Knowledge of OSPF and BGP, OSI Model,NAC (Network Access Control). * Ability to support Wireless Technologies. * Working knowledge of Fortinet (or other) SD WAN technologies. * Working knowledge of Aruba Clearpass, Controllers and Airwave. * Knowledge of Alcatel\-Lucent OmniSwitches (Or Cisco, HP). * Experience with VPN technologies and how they impact various applications. * Experience with other cloud based VC applications. * Experience in dealing with multiple 3rd party WAN providers. * Proven ability to work on their own in an outage situation. * Support experience to users of all levels in busy helpdesk environment. * Hands\-on experience in managing and working in computer rooms. This includes cabling and racks. * Understanding of environmental issues such as air conditioning, UPS, water leak detection * Certifications such as CCNA are a distinctive advantage. At Nostra we value our people. We are a passionate team committed to doing our best, our values are trust, accountability, expertise, people, integrity, and empathy. These values form a central part of our recruitment process. Nostra is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion.

title: Office Administrator
description: We’ve worked hard to build a culture where people feel a genuine sense of belonging, wherever they work in our operations, and whatever their role. Our culture is grounded in our core values and our very strong commitment to delivering every day for our clients and their customers, getting everyone home safely, and caring about your wellbeing. We believe the diverse work we do really matters – whether we are connecting people and businesses to complex fibre networks, supporting large industrial projects and utilities, building modular spaces in which to learn, work or play, or delivering creative events to inspire and entertain. **Responsibilities:** * Adhere to and maintain compliance with all Health and Safety procedures within your work. * Engaging with our customers and clients, assisting them with all queries they have. * Daily and weekly job scheduling * Maintaining the Work Management System * Compiling daily operational reports * Preparation of PowerPoint slides * Engaging with service engineers providing them with support where required * Ad hoc administrative duties **Requirements:** * 1\+ years related experience * Excellent literacy, numeric and IT skills including proficiency in Microsoft Office * Knowledge of Microsoft Excel required * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills * Attention to detail * Experience working in a fast\-paced environment * Decisive, self\-motivated with the ability to represent the company in a professional manner at all times * Innovative, creative and proactive with a solution orientated approach **Benefits:** * Pension plan * Life assurance – 4 times your annual salary * Employee assistance programme * Refer a friend scheme * Maternity pay * Educational assistance * Career progression opportunities * Long Service Awards

title: Online Vehicle Specialist (487)
description: (Initially based at Mercedes\-Benz North Dublin – Navan Road, before relocating to our brand\-new flagship dealership in North City Business Park, North Road, Dublin 11 in Q3 2026\.) MSL Motor Group is seeking a professional, customer\-focused, and motivated Online Vehicle Specialist to join our Mercedes\-Benz North Dublin team during an exciting period of transformation and growth. This newly developed flagship dealership will unite our existing Mercedes\-Benz North Dublin sales operation on the Navan Road with our MSL Service Centre Mercedes\-Benz, dedicated element of our service operation at North Circular Road, bringing both together into a single, purpose\-built, state\-of\-the\-art facility. Built from the ground up under the Mercedes\-Benz MAR20X retail transformation standards, this new location will redefine the aftersales experience, focusing on luxury, trust, ease, and respect at every touchpoint. As an Online Vehicle Specialist, you will play a central role in delivering this experience and will report directly to the Sales Manager. **Role Overview** The Online Vehicle Specialist is responsible for ensuring that every new and used vehicle is professionally presented across all online platforms and digital channels. The role includes photographing and filming vehicles, maintaining accurate vehicle descriptions and specifications, and ensuring that digital showroom displays and multimedia screens are current, operational and aligned with the latest Mercedes\-Benz brand content. This position requires attention to detail, creativity and organisational skills, ensuring every vehicle is presented to the highest possible standard and contributes to generating customer enquiries and sales opportunities. Full training will be provided on all dealership systems and Mercedes\-Benz digital platforms. **Key Responsibilities** **Online Vehicle Management** * Upload all new and used vehicles to the dealership website and associated online advertising platforms. * Ensure vehicle listings are accurate, complete and published promptly. * Update pricing, specifications, features and descriptions as required. * Monitor online vehicle listings to ensure accuracy and consistency across all platforms. * Remove sold vehicles and update stock status in a timely manner. * Maintain the highest standards of online vehicle presentation. **Vehicle Photography \& Video Production** * Photograph all vehicles to a consistent premium standard. * Capture high\-quality interior and exterior images highlighting key features. * Produce vehicle walkaround videos for online advertising and social media. * Ensure photography and video content reflects Mercedes\-Benz brand values. * Organise and manage digital image and video libraries. * Continuously seek opportunities to improve content quality and customer engagement. **Vehicle Content Creation** * Write accurate and engaging vehicle descriptions. * Highlight key selling points, specifications and optional equipment. * Ensure content is grammatically correct, informative and consistent with brand guidelines. * Support marketing initiatives through the creation of vehicle\-related digital content. **Digital Showroom Management** * Manage and update digital "Silent Salesman" displays within the showroom. * Ensure all vehicle information displayed digitally is accurate and current. * Maintain showroom multimedia displays to enhance the customer experience. * Verify that all digital screens are functioning correctly and displaying the appropriate content. * Liaise with internal departments to ensure showroom information remains current. **Mercedes\-Benz Digital Content Management** * Update showroom multimedia screens using the Mercedes\-Benz Digital Networks platform. * Download and deploy the latest Mercedes\-Benz marketing campaigns and video content. * Ensure all digital displays reflect current model launches, campaigns and brand messaging. * Monitor system performance and report any technical issues promptly. * Maintain compliance with Mercedes\-Benz corporate digital standards. **Stock Presentation Support** * Coordinate with the Sales and Vehicle Preparation teams to ensure vehicles are ready for photography. * Maintain consistency in image quality and presentation standards. * Identify vehicles requiring updated photography or revised online content. * Assist in ensuring showroom vehicles are displayed to the highest standard. **Quality Assurance** * Conduct regular audits of online vehicle listings. * Ensure pricing and specifications are accurate. * Check that images, videos and descriptions meet dealership quality standards. * Correct any discrepancies promptly. * Monitor competitor online presentation and identify opportunities for improvement. **Essential** **Skills \& Experience** * Excellent attention to detail. * Organisational and time management skills. * Good computer literacy and confidence using digital platforms. * Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities. * Positive attitude and willingness to learn. * Full clean driving licence. **About MSL Motor Group** MSL Motor Group, a family\-owned business since 1959, sells and services Mercedes\-Benz, Mazda, ŠKODA, BYD, XPENG and smart cars. Their mission is to achieve complete customer satisfaction through passion, innovation, and modern dealerships. They have several sales dealerships and service centres in Dublin and Cork, including a new dedicated Mercedes\-Benz showroom and service centre in Cabinteely. Founded by Stephen O'Flaherty, a pioneer in Irish motoring, the O'Flaherty family has been involved in the motor industry since 1950\. Stephen secured the Volkswagen franchise for Ireland in 1950 and the UK in 1953\. The first Volkswagen built outside Germany was assembled at their Shelbourne Road premises. In 1954, Stephen obtained the Mercedes\-Benz distribution rights for Ireland, which the family still manages. The Naas Road facility in Dublin serves as the headquarters for both MSL Motor Group and Mercedes\-Benz Ireland. **About Mercedes Benz** Mercedes\-Benz Group AG is one of the world's most successful automotive companies. It is one of the leading global suppliers of high\-end luxury passenger cars and premium vans. As a pioneer of automotive engineering, Mercedes\-Benz sees shaping the future of mobility in a safe and sustainable way as both a motivation and an obligation. The company's focus therefore remains on innovative and green technologies as well as on safe and superior vehicles that both captivate and inspire. If you are ready to take your career to the next level and join our luxury brand here in Mercedes\-Benz North Dublin, apply now. Please submit a cover letter outlining your availability and salary expectation, together with a comprehensive Curriculum Vitae.

title: Overnight - Residence Experience Associate
description: **Role Summary** We are seeking a motivated Overnight Resident Experience Associate to join our team at Glass Bottle, a premier Build\-to\-Rent (BTR) property located in Sandymount Strand, Co. Dublin. This is an ideal opportunity for someone looking to progress their career in the growing BTR industry, working for a company that is expanding its footprint in Ireland. The successful candidate, reporting to the Property Manager, will be integral to daily property operations, focusing on resident relations, health and safety, cleanliness and security. This highly visible role will involve regular interaction with residents and visitors helping ensure high standards in resident engagement and satisfaction. We’re seeking candidates with strong experience in customer engagement. This job would ideally suit candidates from hospitality roles like: Security Operative, Night Auditor, Assistant Night Manager / Night Manager. CompassRock has a dedicated and comprehensive training and certification program, and this role will have the potential to progress into more managerial role over time. Some of the training programs include The Property Institute’s Build to Rent course and Managing Safely Qualifications. **Company Overview** CompassRock provides asset and property management services to the rental housing sector. Originally founded in the U.S., the company has expanded to the UK and is rapidly growing its portfolio of Build\-to\-Rent (BTR) assets across England and Europe. CompassRock is in an exciting growth phase, actively acquiring and developing new assets at a fast pace. With an entrepreneurial and innovative culture embedded in its DNA, the company values the dynamic, forward\-thinking individuals it employs. As CompassRock continues to expand, there will be abundant opportunities for career advancement and professional development. **Key Responsibilities** **Customer Service \& Resident Relations:** * Policy and Standards Adherence: Uphold the company’s customer service standards to deliver high\-quality service and resident satisfaction. * Issue Resolution: Address resident complaints and actively troubleshoot problems to take ownership in challenging situations, escalating issues to the Property Manager when necessary. * Resident Engagement Initiatives: Support the planning and execution of community\-building activities that foster positive resident relations and engagement. * Resident Support: Provide residents with appropriate information throughout the tenancy lifespan, including providing renewal information, ensuring legal requirements are met and answering any questions that might occur during the tenancy lifespan. * Relationship Building: Cultivate strong relationships with residents, ensuring professional and prompt communication. Regularly engage with residents and prospective tenants to build strong rapport and trust. * Leasing Support: Provide administrative support to the Leasing team, as required. * Enquiries: Greet and assist prospective tenants and to qualify and register enquiries and to book viewing appointments at our properties (if required). * Viewings: Conduct informative and engaging viewings to prospective tenants being well\-equipped to answer any questions regarding the property (if required). * Administrative Support: Working with the leasing team to help maintain accurate and organised records of leases, applications, and tenant correspondence. **Operational Activities** * Property Support: Contribute to maintaining a safe and attractive property for residents. Facilities Management Support: Assist the Property Manager with managing projects, overseeing outsourced services for building maintenance, and ensuring timely, high\-quality completion of tasks. * Cleanliness: Complete overnight cleaning of the amenity spaces, ensuring that the property is “resident ready” each day. * ‘Stock’ Coordination: Collaborate with the property manager and procurement team to ensure adequate stock of goods and services for smooth property operations. Completing stock checks on a monthly basis. * Inspections: Conduct / support inventories, move\-in and move\-out inspections, documenting property conditions. * Administrative Support: Assist the Property Manager with all other general administrative duties, as required. **Targets And KPIs** * Work diligently to meet specified targets and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) set by the management team. **Personal Qualities** * Attention to Detail: Demonstrated focus on quality and compliance in previous work or education (desired). * Proactive and Initiative\-Driven: Able to foresee potential issues and address them before they escalate (desired). Sharing suggestions on how to improve efficiencies, encourage proactivity and support your colleagues. * Flexibility: Ability to adapt to changing circumstances, particularly in a fast\-paced property environment (desired). * Positive, 'can\-do' Attitude: A willingness to learn from, and provide support to, other team members, while delivering top levels of service to all residents (essential). **Skills** * Communication Skills: Both written and verbal, for interacting with residents, staff, and external stakeholders (essential) * Problem\-Solving: Strong abilities in addressing resident disputes (essential). * Customer Service: Demonstrated experience in delivering exceptional customer service (essential). * Computer Literacy: While training on our property management systems will be provided, we require that the applicant be proficient with Microsoft Office and Outlook and have an aptitude for learning new software (essential). **Qualifications (desired)** * Bachelor’s degree in business administration, or a related field (desired) * Full clean Irish driver’s licence **Remuneration/ Bonuses / Benefits** * Base salary:€37,400 * Bonus: Discretionary Bonus Scheme * Benefits: Private health insurance; Pension Scheme; Cycle to Work Scheme; Company Sick Pay. **Working Hours** * 5\-day working week (40 hours) including weekends (Saturday and Sunday). * This role includes daytime shift work (between 22:30 – 07:30\) **Location:** This is a client facing role and must be based on site at the rostered location(primarily at Glass Bottle, Sandymount Strand, Dublin 4 but may be required to work at other locations as required by the business). **Equal Opportunities Statement** We are an Equal Opportunities Employer, committed to creating an inclusive, diverse, and equitable workplace. We provide equal opportunities for everyone and offer reasonable adjustments to ensure all candidates have a fair chance during the recruitment process.
